title: Commis Chef
description: Be Yourself – Be a Bold Team Player – Be Leonardo Once you’re part of the team, you’re part of the Leonardo community of over 55 hotels across the UK \& Ireland! The Commis Chef will support the smooth running and objectives of the Kitchen. All members of the department work together to create memorable moments for guests, in line with both company and hotel\-specific service standards, and collaborate closely with other departments within the hotel. You will act as an ambassador for your own hotel and the wider Leonardo Hotels estate, demonstrating the company vision, mission and values. At all times, you will treat others around you with respect, dignity and fairness. As a Commis Chef you’ll be right in the heart of the hotel, so we want you to take pride in everything you’ll be doing to support the Head Chef and keep things running like clockwork. Take a deep breath because you’ll be stepping into a busy role! You’ll thrive under pressure and see a future working with food, and with Leonardo Hotels of course, whilst working as part of a vibrant team to exceed our guests’ expectations. If you’re our ideal Commis Chef, you will: * Absolutely love the Kitchen environment and working within a team of Chefs to deliver excellent food and service * Have a willingness to learn, adapt and deliver to the high standard of food our guests expect * Be passionate about your working area and responsibilities * Be keen to step up when the situation demands * Have an eagle eye for attention to detail * Aspire to grow with us – with our amazing Junior Chef Programme, you can take your skills and career to the next level Some of the perks our Commis Chef could enjoy include:* Special rates on Leonardo Hotel rooms across the UK \& Europe * Company\-wide recognition scheme: you could earn vouchers to spend on a wide range of high street shops * Talent referral scheme * Thank You Week: from ice cream trucks to yoga classes and lots in between! * Wellbeing Calendar * Ongoing job\-related training programmes with clear paths for progression We’re committed to creating a workplace where every individual—regardless of background, identity, or lived experience—is welcomed, valued, and respected for who they are. Belonging means embracing our differences and ensuring everyone feels seen, heard, and empowered. Why come join us as a Commis Chef? We look after our colleagues just as well as we look after our guests. Our regular Learning Bites, “Come Join Us Guest Care” training and Wellbeing webinars all help your progress with us. With support on tap, top\-notch practical training and clear progression you’ll be on track for a brilliant future in Hospitality!

title: Compliance Analyst - Surveillance
description: A leading financial services organisation in Dublin is seeking a Compliance Analyst to join their dedicated Compliance Department. * Join a well\-established financial services organisation in Dublin with a strong reputation for nurturing talent and providing comprehensive training opportunities. * Play a key role in maintaining regulatory standards by conducting email reviews, supporting investigations, and enhancing compliance policies within a supportive team environment. **What You'll Do** * Conduct thorough reviews of email communications for trading groups, ensuring all correspondence adheres to regulatory requirements and internal standards. * Perform detailed searches related to internal investigations, collaborating closely with colleagues across departments to gather relevant information. * Prepare comprehensive weekly and monthly memos summarising findings from email surveillance reviews, presenting clear insights to senior management. * Assist in the development and enhancement of compliance policies and procedures, adapting them to reflect changes in business operations, regulations, and emerging issues. * Stay up\-to\-date with relevant regulatory developments and changes, proactively sharing knowledge with the wider team to ensure collective understanding. * Participate actively in other compliance\-related projects as needed, demonstrating flexibility and willingness to support organisational goals. * Work collaboratively with colleagues across departments to build effective working relationships that facilitate smooth project delivery. * Utilise technological applications and tools efficiently to streamline compliance processes and improve overall department productivity. * Contribute positively to a culture of continuous improvement by identifying areas for process optimisation within the compliance function. **What You Bring** * Bachelor’s degree (preferably in Business, Finance, Economics or related field) with at least three years’ experience in compliance roles within financial services. * Prior experience at a Registered Investment Adviser or Broker\-Dealer is highly desirable for this position. * Strong understanding of regulatory agencies such as Central Bank of Ireland, FCA, SEC, CFTC, MAS along with familiarity with key regulations including Investment Advisers Act 1940, Securities Act 1933, Financial Services and Markets Act 2000 (FSMA). * Excellent team player who thrives on co\-operation and collaboration within diverse groups. * Ability to prioritise tasks effectively in environments characterised by constant change and evolving demands. * Entrepreneurial mindset combined with proven ability to manage projects independently while remaining responsive to team needs. * Exceptional writing skills paired with analytical abilities that enable you to produce clear reports and solve complex problems. * Proficiency with Microsoft Excel, Word, PowerPoint as well as other technological applications relevant to compliance work. * Broad knowledge of financial products and the wider financial services industry is considered advantageous for this role. * Strong interpersonal skills enabling you to build dependable relationships across departments. Robert Walters Operations Limited is an employment business and employment agency and welcomes applications from all candidates

title: Construction Management Graduate
description: **Role:** In this role, you’ll support project managers and site teams in planning, coordinating, and delivering construction projects safely, on time, and within budget. **Key Responsibilities** * Assist in project planning and scheduling * Support site management and daily operations * Monitor project progress and report on performance * Help manage budgets, costs, and procurement Coordinate with subcontractors, suppliers, and clients * Ensure compliance with health \& safety regulations * Review drawings, specifications, and contracts * Participate in site meetings and documentation * Help resolve on\-site issues and delays **Qualifications \& Experience** * Bachelor’s degree in Construction Management, Civil Engineering, Quantity Surveying, or a related discipline * Strong understanding of construction methods, materials, and project lifecycles * Ability to interpret technical drawings, specifications, and contract documents * Effective communication skills, with the ability to liaise with site teams, subcontractors, and clients * Good organisational and time\-management abilities, capable of handling multiple tasks and deadlines * Problem\-solving skills with a proactive and practical approach to on\-site challenges * Attention to detail, particularly in relation to quality control and health \& safety compliance * Team\-oriented mindset with the ability to work independently when required * Basic commercial awareness, including cost control and budgeting principles * Proficiency in Microsoft Office (especially Excel) and familiarity with construction software such as AutoCAD or project planning tools * Knowledge of health and safety regulations and a commitment to maintaining safe working environments * Willingness to learn, adapt, and develop within a fast\-paced construction environment Pay: €35,000\.00\-€45,000\.00 per year Benefits: * On\-site parking Work authorisation: * Ireland (required) Work Location: In person

title: Controls Engineer
description: **Field Service Engineer, Controls** Location: Dublin and with travel across Ireland with some international trips. We are looking for an experienced Field Service Engineer to commission, optimise and support automation and control systems on bespoke production equipment. This is a predominantly site\-based, hands\-on role working directly on customer machinery and control systems, often with minimal supervision. You will ensure equipment is installed, started up and running reliably, while maintaining high technical standards and a professional, customer\-focused approach. **Key Responsibilities** * Configure, modify and support PLC, HMI and related PC\-based control software in line with customer process and safety requirements * Commission, fault\-find and optimise automation and control systems at client sites to achieve agreed performance and uptime targets * Diagnose electrical, pneumatic and control issues using schematics, manuals and diagnostic tools, implementing robust and sustainable fixes * Plan and execute site work independently or as part of a wider project team, coordinating with customer production schedules * Align on\-site activities with customer objectives, ensuring work is clearly planned, communicated and documented * Share operational and troubleshooting knowledge with maintenance and operations teams, providing training and guidance where needed * Recommend tools, spare parts or equipment improvements to enhance reliability, maintainability and performance * Make balanced recommendations on repair versus replacement, focusing on cost\-effective, long\-term solutions * Lead and coordinate site activities involving internal colleagues, customer personnel and third\-party vendors * Produce clear visit reports and service documentation, tracking open actions through to resolution * Participate in an on\-call rota to support customers outside standard hours when required * Communicate effectively with internal stakeholders and customer contacts at all levels * Support warranty and non\-warranty service activities in line with company processes * Work safely at all times, following site rules and company health and safety procedures * Represent the company professionally on customer sites as a trusted technical point of contact **Qualifications** * Degree or technical qualification in Electrical, Automation or Control Engineering, or a closely related discipline * Experience with databases, robotics or vision systems is advantageous but not essential **Experience and Skills** * Several years’ experience in a practical automation or control systems role, typically 4\+ years * Strong background developing, modifying and troubleshooting PLC and HMI applications, ideally on Allen\-Bradley and or Siemens platforms * Solid understanding of electrical, pneumatic and multi\-axis control systems * Confident reading and interpreting technical drawings, wiring diagrams and system documentation * Good working knowledge of Microsoft Word and Excel for reporting and documentation * Demonstrated ability to learn new systems quickly and troubleshoot complex automated equipment * Willingness and flexibility to travel extensively, sometimes at short notice * Experience within life sciences or pharmaceutical manufacturing is beneficial but not required **About You** You are a clear and confident communicator who builds effective working relationships with engineers, operators, vendors and management. You stay calm under pressure, organise your workload effectively and take ownership of issues through to resolution. You enjoy being on site, solving real\-world technical problems and delivering a high standard of service without close supervision.

title: Cyber Security Analyst
description: We are seeking a **GRC (Governance, Risk, and Compliance) Analyst** to join our client’s security team in Dublin. This role will be crucial in a fast\-paced environment where you'll be responsible for ensuring our security posture aligns with key regulatory frameworks and industry best practices. The ideal candidate will have deep expertise in **ISO 27001** , **NIST** , and **DORA** . **Responsibilities** * **Compliance \& Governance:** Lead the implementation and maintenance of security frameworks, specifically **ISO 27001** , **NIST CSF** , and the new **DORA** regulation. * **Risk Management:** Conduct detailed risk assessments and gap analyses to identify vulnerabilities and compliance gaps against specified frameworks. * **Audits:** Coordinate and support internal and external audits, including preparing evidence and documentation for auditors. You will be the point of contact for audit inquiries. * **Policy \& Procedure:** Develop, update, and manage security policies, standards, and procedures to ensure they align with the latest regulatory requirements. * **Reporting:** Create and present clear, actionable reports on our compliance status, risk posture, and remediation efforts to both technical teams and senior leadership. * **Remediation:** Track and facilitate the remediation of identified control gaps, collaborating with various teams to ensure timely resolution. **Skills \& Qualifications** * **Experience:** At least 3\-5 years of experience in a GRC, information security, or IT audit role. * **Technical Expertise:** A comprehensive and practical understanding of **ISO 27001** and its implementation. Experience with **NIST frameworks** (e.g., CSF, SP 800 series) is essential. * **DORA Knowledge:** A strong understanding of the **Digital Operational Resilience Act (DORA)** is a key requirement. You must know what it entails and how to apply it within a financial or IT services organization. * **Certifications (Preferred):** Relevant professional certifications are highly desirable, such as **ISO 27001 Lead Implementer/Auditor** , **CRISC** , **CISA** , or **CISSP** . * **Communication:** Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills. You must be able to translate complex security and compliance requirements into a business context for non\-technical audiences. * **Problem\-Solving:** Strong analytical and problem\-solving abilities, with a meticulous attention to detail.

title: Cyber Security Engineer
description: **Dublin \| Hybrid (2 days onsite)** **€40\-65k With Benefits (DOE)** We are currently assisting a client in a search for a Cyber Security Engineer to join a growing security function supporting cloud\-based insurance technology used by leading global providers. This is a hands\-on security role for someone technical, curious, and eager to grow across all areas of security including, appsec, cloud, vulnerability, operations and engineering. You’ll work across vulnerability management, security tooling, threat analysis, and secure development \- partnering closely with engineering, DevOps, and infrastructure teams to investigate issues, improve controls, and drive practical security improvements. This is a strong fit for someone from a software, infrastructure, or DevOps background looking to specialise in security. **What You’ll Do** * Investigate vulnerabilities and work with technical teams to drive remediation * Support security projects across AppSec, cloud, and endpoint hardening * Help implement and improve tooling across areas like DAST, monitoring, and vulnerability management * Partner with developers and engineers on secure design, risk reduction, and technical controls * Support threat modelling, security reviews, and technical security initiatives across the SDLC * Work across Microsoft and cloud environments including Azure, Entra ID, and Intune **What They’re Looking For** * Technical background in software, DevOps, infrastructure, or security engineering ideally 2\-5 years * Strong understanding of how modern applications and cloud environments work * Strong knowledge of cloud security (Azure), IAM, Zero‑Trust. * Experience with SIEM/SOAR and vulnerability management. * Knowledge of SCA/SAST/DAST tools and secure coding. * Ability to speak with developers, challenge assumptions, and solve technical problems * Scripting, automation, or development exposure (Java knowledge a plus) * Curious mindset, strong logic, and willingness to learn across multiple security domains **Why join** * Broad, hands\-on security role with strong learning scope * Opportunity to grow into a key security position * Hybrid working (2 days onsite, Dublin city centre) * Excellent benefits and working in forward thinking company.

title: Data & BI Engineer
description: **Job Title: Data \& BI Engineer** **Job Description:** Our client, a highly regarded and rapidly growing financial services organisation, is seeking a talented Data \& BI Engineer to join their high\-performing technology team, delivering innovative data solutions that drive strategic decision\-making. **Job Responsibilities** * Design, build, and optimise scalable data pipelines and data models to support reporting and analytics requirements * Analyse system logs and datasets to troubleshoot and resolve data\-related issues across multiple platforms * Develop and enhance BI solutions, ensuring high\-quality data visualisation and reporting outputs * Collaborate with cross\-functional teams including Product, QA, and DevOps to deliver robust data solutions * Contribute to continuous improvement initiatives across development, deployment, and data processes * Participate in architectural discussions, providing input into system and data design decisions * Document technical solutions, workflows, and processes to support knowledge sharing and future scalability * Support the development of internal tools and automation to improve efficiency and reduce manual effort **Experience Required** * 4–6 years’ experience in a Data Engineering, BI Engineering, or similar role * Strong experience in SQL, including complex query development and performance optimisation * Proven background in data warehousing, data modelling, and ETL/ELT processes * Experience working with large\-scale datasets and delivering data\-driven solutions * Exposure to cloud platforms such as AWS or Azure * Experience implementing testing strategies including unit, integration, and end\-to\-end testing * Strong understanding of software development best practices including code reviews and documentation * Ability to troubleshoot and resolve moderately complex technical issues **Desirable Skills** * Experience with tools and technologies such as Power BI, Snowflake, PostgreSQL, or MS SQL Server * Knowledge of modern data architecture concepts such as data mesh or data products * Familiarity with AWS services such as Glue, S3, Spark, and EventBridge * Experience with programming languages such as Python or C\# * Understanding of CI/CD pipelines and DevOps practices * Exposure to industry sectors such as financial services is advantageous **Educational Requirements** * Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, or a related discipline **Working Hours \& Benefits** * Competitive salary package * Flexible and hybrid working arrangements * Pension contribution * Annual bonus scheme * Ongoing professional development and training opportunities **How to Apply** To apply for this role, please submit your updated CV. If your skills and experience match job requirements, you will be contacted to discuss your application in detail. **Similar Roles Open** As a specialised recruitment agency, we also have several other permanent and contract IT roles available. Visit our website for more information or explore additional opportunities.

title: Data Quality Support Analyst
description: **Line of Service** Internal Firm Services **Industry/Sector** Not Applicable **Specialism** IFS \- Internal Firm Services \- Other **Management Level** Associate **Job Description \& Summary** The CDO team provides data governance \& compliance, data quality management and data management \& enablement services. A data quality support analyst is required to support across data quality operational services and initiatives. They will play a key support role, working closely with the Data Quality Management Lead and the wider CDO team to help deliver data quality initiatives, maintain data quality standards, and support the implementation of data governance activities across the firm. **Key Responsibilities;** * Manage and maintain master data in alignment with global standard s * Act as a data steward, treating master \& reference data as a strategic asset. * Conduct root cause analysis and propose actionable solutions for data related issues. * Manage ServiceNow requests and ensure timely resolution within SLA. * Maintain and update process documentation and procedures. * Prepare training materials and support in the delivery of training to offshore teams . * Participate in system testing ensuring accurate alignment of master data across platforms. * Remediation and cleansing of master \& reference data Provide subject matter expertise on data within your domain and territory. * Promote best practices in data management and ensure adherence to global policies. **Experience \& Qualifications** * Minimum 4 years’ experience in a data management, operations, or a qualit y\-focused role. * Third\-level qualification required ; a degree in data management or related field is desirable. * Familiarity with data quality frameworks (e.g., DAMA\-DMBOK) desirable. * Experience in quality control, cleansing and remediation. * Proven ability to manage multiple priorities . * Experience working in high\-performing teams and delivering to deadlines . * Foundational project management experience including stakeholder management , task planning, and milestone tracking. **Skills** * Excellent attention to detail and commitment to data accuracy. * Strong analytical and problem\-solving skills. * Strong verbal and written communication skills including tailored communications to different stakeholders. * Critical thinking skills, asks clarifying questions, i dentifies gaps and challenges assumptions. * Comfortable escalating issues and suggesting improvements. * Curious, proactive, and self\-motivated with a strong team ethic. * Demonstrates initiative in seeking out information, staying current with processes and procedures, and quickly adapting to new knowledge. * Ability to build and maintain relationships across teams . * Adapt s well to fee d back demonst r ating a commitment to self\-development. * Organised, with strong prioritisation and time management skills. * Enthusiastic about data quality and its role in driving business value. * Adaptable and resilient in a changing environment. **Unlock your potential with PwC Ireland** W e believe that challenges are better solved together! We’re inspiring and empowering our people to change the world. Powered by the latest technology, you’ll be a part of amazing teams encouraged to collaborate and innovate in a way that creates powerful solutions and makes a positive impact. This purpose\-led work, and our continuous development and encouragement, will help unlock your potential and take your career to the next level. **Enjoy PwC’s perks** We reward your impact, and support your wellbeing, through a competitive compensation package, inclusive employee benefits and flexibility programs that will help you thrive in work and life. Learn more about us at Life@PwC . ( https://www.pwc.ie/careers\-ie/life\-at\-pwc.html ). **Being appreciated for being you** Our most valuable asset is our people and we grow stronger as we learn from one another. We are an equal opportunity employer and we value diversity. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, color , national origin, sex, gender, gender expression, sexual orientation, age, marital status, veteran status, or disability status . We believe this so much that we have signed up for the Business in the Community Elevate Pledge . ( https://www.bitc.ie/the\-leaders\-group\-on\-sustainability/inclusive\-workplace\-pledge/ ) You can learn more about our culture of belonging and explore our range of inclusive programmes, initiatives, employee resource groups and more at www.pwc.ie . Avoid the confidence gap; you do not have to match all the listed requirements exactly to apply, we’d love to hear from you! We will ensure that individuals with disabilities are provided reasonable accommodation to participate in the job application or interview process, to perform essential job functions, and to receive other benefits and privileges of employment. Please email us for more information. **Required Skills** **Optional Skills** Accepting Feedback, Accepting Feedback, Active Listening, Azure DevOps Server, Communication, Database Administration, Database Performance Management, Database Quality Assurance, Database Troubleshooting, Data Error Checking, Data Optimization, Data Profiling, Data Quality, Data Quality Assessment, Data Quality Assurance Testing, Data Quality Automation, Data Science Troubleshooting, Data Validation, Data Warehousing Quality Assurance, Emotional Regulation, Empathy, Inclusion, Intellectual Curiosity, Key Performance Indicators (KPIs), Metadata Management {\+ 5 more} **Desired Languages** *(If blank, desired languages not specified)* **Travel Requirements** Not Specified **Available for Work Visa Sponsorship?** No **Government Clearance Required?** No **Job Posting End Date**

title: Database Administrator (DBA)
description: **About Air Apps** ================== At Air Apps, we believe in thinking bigger—and moving faster. We’re a family\-founded company on a mission to create the world’s first AI\-powered Personal \& Entrepreneurial Resource Planner (PRP), and we need your passion and ambition to help us change how people plan, work, and live. Born in Lisbon, Portugal in 2018—and now with offices in both Lisbon and San Francisco—we’ve remained self\-funded while reaching over 100 million downloads worldwide. Our long\-term focus drives us to challenge the status quo every day, pushing the boundaries of AI\-driven solutions that truly make a difference. Here, you’ll be a creative force, shaping products that empower people across the globe. Join us on this journey to redefine resource management—and change lives along the way. **The Role** ============ As a **Database Administrator (DBA)** at Air Apps, you will be responsible for **managing, optimizing, and securing large\-scale, complex databases**. You will work with engineering teams to **design, implement, and maintain high\-performance, scalable, and resilient database systems** that power our AI\-driven platform. Your role will involve **database tuning, backup and recovery strategies, monitoring, and ensuring data integrity** while working with structured and unstructured data sources. * This is a **fully onsite position**, based at our office in Lisbon, where you will collaborate closely with cross\-functional teams in person and contribute to a dynamic and fast\-paced environment. **We are open to support with relocation efforts.** **Responsibilities** ==================== * Design, implement, and maintain **high\-performance database systems** to support large\-scale applications. * Optimize **database queries, indexing strategies, and partitioning** to enhance performance. * Monitor database health, ensuring **high availability, scalability, and disaster recovery**. * Manage and fine\-tune **SQL and NoSQL databases** (e.g., PostgreSQL, MySQL, MongoDB, Redis, DynamoDB). * Implement **database security policies**, encryption, and access control to protect sensitive data. * Automate database **backups, replication, and failover mechanisms**. * Work closely with developers to optimize **queries, schema design, and data modeling**. * Perform **capacity planning** and monitor database growth trends. * Ensure compliance with **data governance, GDPR, and other regulatory requirements**. * Stay updated with the latest **database technologies, trends, and best practices**. **Requirements** ================ * Around **4\+ years** of experience as a **Database Administrator (DBA) or similar role**. * Expertise in **relational databases (PostgreSQL, MySQL, SQL Server)** and/or **NoSQL databases (MongoDB, Redis, Cassandra, DynamoDB)**. * Strong knowledge of **database optimization, indexing, partitioning, and replication**. * Experience in **performance tuning, query optimization, and troubleshooting slow queries**. * Familiarity with **database backup, recovery, failover, and disaster recovery strategies**. * Experience with **cloud database services** (AWS RDS, Aurora, Google Cloud Spanner, Azure SQL). * Knowledge of **scripting for automation** (Python, Bash, SQL scripts). * Understanding of **data security best practices**, encryption, and compliance requirements. * Experience working with **large, complex datasets and high\-traffic applications**. * Ability to work with **DevOps teams** to integrate databases into CI/CD workflows. **What benefits are we offering?** ================================== * **Apple hardware** ecosystem for work. * **Annual Bonus** * **Top\-tier Health and Life Insurance** for peace of mind. * **Transportation Budget** to support your commute needs. * **Coverflex** benefits package for meal allowances, well\-being, and more. * **Childcare** support. * **Air Conference** \- an opportunity to meet the team, collaborate, and grow together. * **Pension Fund** to support your long\-term financial planning. * **Urban Sports Club** membership to keep you active. * **Meals 100% free** at the hub. **Diversity \& Inclusion** ========================== At Air Apps, we are committed to fostering a diverse, inclusive, and equitable workplace. We enthusiastically welcome applicants from all backgrounds, experiences, and perspectives. We celebrate diversity in all its forms and believe that varied voices and experiences make us stronger. **Application Disclaimer** ========================== At Air Apps, we value transparency and integrity in our hiring process. Applicants must submit their own work without any AI\-generated assistance. Any use of AI in application materials, assessments, or interviews will result in disqualification. Compensation Range: €55K \- €69K

title: Demand Planner
description: **About Us** Home to the iconic Instantor® Brand, Sanbra Fyffe is a market leader in the manufacturing, wholesale and distribution of plumbing supplies to the builder’s merchants trade for 90 years. At Instantor, our values aren’t just words on a page \- they shape how we work, support each other, and deliver for our customers. We’re looking for someone who shares these values and wants to thrive in a collaborative, customer\-focused environment. **Our Values** Teamwork: All departments within the business act as one team to make Sanbra Fyffe the best it can be. Customer Focus: We commit to meet and exceed our customer’s expectations, by placing the customer at the centre of all our decisions. Quality \& Reliability: We focus on doing things the right way, the first time; delivering on quality, on time and on cost. Expertise: We develop and manufacture solutions to provide our customers with maximum value. **Role Purpose** Due to strong growth within the business, we are expanding our Purchasing Team and looking to hire an experienced Demand Planner. This is an excellent opportunity and a fantastic time for an ambitious individual to join a high\-paced, growing Purchasing Department at the company’s HQ in Santry, Dublin. This role will play a critical role in improving demand planning and forecasting practices, acting as a key link between commercial and operational functions. The Demand Planner will own the integrity and output of newly implemented demand planning tool, to enable the purchasing team to make confident, timely decisions and improve overall stock availability. **Key Responsibilities** * Maintain and uphold the integrity of the third\-party demand planning tool and act as key user, ensuring that the output of the tool is trustworthy and actionable for the purchasers * Participate in forecast review and validation process, provide clear reporting on forecast accuracy and act as a key player in strengthening S\&OP practices into the business * Refine sales forecasts for existing products, collecting, checking and analysing all necessary information to increase overall forecasting accuracy and service level * Lead out\-of\-stock reporting and root cause analysis to minimize lost sales * Pro\-actively identify future inventory risks and opportunities and liaise with Purchasing, Operations and Sales teams with actionable recommendations * Manage phase\-in and phase\-out implications on forecast of obsolescent ranges and new product introductions, translating these insights into the demand planning tool * Acts as a forecasting interface between purchasing and sales departments, translating commercial insights into forecast adjustments in the demand planning tool * Work closely with Operations and Sales teams to refine inventory level targets in 3PL * Work within an experienced and expanding Purchasing department **Key Requirements** * Relevant third level qualification; preferably in Supply Chain Management, Business or related fields * A minimum of 3 years’ experience in demand planning and S\&OP input * Proficiency in forecasting tools and forecasting methodologies * Proficiency in Microsoft Office suite (Word, Excel, PowerPoint etc.) and reporting tools such as Power BI * Experience working with SQL based ERP systems * Proactive attitude and strong communication skills, comfortable collaborating with senior stakeholders across the business * Detail\-oriented with a structured, process\-driven mindset * Team player * A desire to learn about our products, systems and customers **Benefits Package** * Competitive Salary * Employer Pension Contribution from start date * Employee Assistance Program (for you, your partner/spouse, and dependents 16\+) * Staff Discounts * Employer funded education support. Learn more at Instantor.ie At Instantor we are strongly committed to providing equal employment opportunities for all and we encourage applications from people of all backgrounds, experiences, and perspectives. If you don’t meet every requirement but are excited about the role, we’d still like to hear from you. We are also committed to providing an accessible recruitment process. If you require any accommodations or adjustments at any stage, please let us know. *We do not require assistance from Recruitment Agencies at this time.* Job Type: Permanent Pay: €52,000\.00\-€55,000\.00 per year Benefits: * Bike to work scheme * Company pension * Employee assistance program * Employee discount * On\-site parking Work Location: In person

title: Dental Hygienist
description: Maypark Dental is a modern private practice seeking a friendly, patient\-focused Dental Hygienist to join our team **TWO days per week** You’ll work in a newly fitted surgery with a brand\-new dental chair and up\-to\-date equipment. **What We Offer** * A modern, fully equipped surgery with a new dental chair (dedicated hygiene surgery one day per week). * Supportive, friendly team in a well\-established private practice. * Competitive remuneration based on experience. * Opportunity to work with a loyal and growing patient base. * Professional environment with high standards of care Job Types: Part\-time, Permanent, Graduate Pay: €50,000\.00\-€80,000\.00 per year Benefits: * Company pension * Employee discount * On\-site parking * Sick pay Work Location: In person

title: Designer– Women’s Accessories
description: **Designer– Women’s Accessories** Because your ideas are our style. Design, our way. As a Designer at Primark, you will plan a critical role in the delivery of the product strategy in Primark. You will drive the success of your department by identifying key seasonal trends, working with suppliers to develop best in class product and alongside B\&M, strategically work to build and deliver well executed and exciting new ranges for our target customers. You will work with teams across design to identify and deliver the newest and latest trends for our Global market. You will deliver colour direction and build seasonally appropriate colour palettes, identify key shapes and graphic newness, and drive fabric innovation. You will be a collaborative and strategic designer, working with B\&M, sourcing and our technical team, to seek out new opportunities of growth, ways to better the product and drive our LFL, in line with Primark’s strategic vision. You will be a well trusted member of the BMD community within Primark. You will be seen as a leader that engages and inspires the junior members of our community. Driving collaboration across the wider Primark teams, to enhance our product offering and position on the high street. **What You’ll Do as a Designer** We want you to feel challenged and inspired. Here, you’ll develop your skills across a range of responsibilities: * Research and shape globally relevant trend direction for women’s accessories, using forecasting tools, data insights and customer analytics to inform commercial ranges. * Lead seasonal colour direction and drive innovation across materials, trims, hardware and construction to deliver differentiated product. * Own the end‑to‑end design and development process across bags, footwear and accessories, from concept through final approval. * Build commercially balanced, customer‑focused ranges aligned to brand DNA, category strategy and clear product architecture. * Lead, manage and mentor a design team, setting high standards for product quality, pace and ways of working. * Collaborate closely with Buying, Merchandising, Technical, Sourcing, suppliers and Brand teams to deliver consistent, high‑quality product execution. **Key Interactions and Engagements as part of this role:** As Designer, you will work closely with Primark’s Buying and Merchandising teams, as well as collaborating with other Design teams across all product categories to ensure alignment and a unified design vision. You will represent your categories and present ranges for sign\-off at leadership meetings. You will also represent your product areas across working groups and forums, contributing to cross\-functional alignment and decision\-making. **What You’ll Get** People are at the heart of what we do here, so it’s essential we provide you with the right environment to perform at your very best. Let’s talk lifestyle. Let’s talk lifestyle: Healthcare, pension, and potential bonus. 27 days of leave, plus bank holidays and if you want, you can buy 5 more. Because Primark is all about tailoring to you, we offer Tax Saver Tickets, fitness centre, and a subsidised cafeteria. **What You’ll Bring** We want every person in our organisation to feel like they’re making a key contribution. Here are some of the experiences and skills you’ll need for the role: * Extensive experience in accessories design, with a minimum of 7 years in design roles and proven people‑management or team‑lead responsibility. * Strong specialist knowledge across accessories, footwear or bags, with deep understanding of materials, construction and end‑to‑end product development. * A track record of delivering commercially successful, high‑volume ranges by translating seasonal trends into customer‑focused product. * Excellent leadership, coaching and mentoring capabilities, with the ability to influence, develop and inspire design teams. * Strong stakeholder and supplier management skills, supported by confident communication, presentation ability and cross‑functional collaboration. * A proactive, well‑organised and accountable approach, thriving in fast‑paced environments while managing multiple priorities with sound judgement and integrity. Excited? Good. Because it’s energising to put your skills to work, in a growing global business committed to encouraging people to express themselves – and feel a sense of belonging. Apply to be part of Primark’s future, today… and enjoy career growth, our way. **About Primark** Primark is an international clothing retailer employing more than 80,000 colleagues across 18 countries in Europe and the US. Founded in Ireland in 1969 under the Penneys brand, Primark aims to create maximum joy at minimum cost – less money and less impact on the planet. Primark continues to expand across new and existing markets with the aim of reaching 530 stores by the end of 2026\. Every person impacts the culture at Primark. Our values: Caring (we always strive to put people first), Dynamic (we bravely push the boundaries to stay ahead) and Together (we learn more, laugh more and achieve more as a team) outline the behaviours we expect of our people as we work with each other, our customers, our suppliers and our partners. Let’s see what impact you can make! **REQ ID:** JR\-8030

title: DevOps Automation Platform Engineer
description: An extraordinarily talented group of individuals work together every day to drive TNS' success, from both professional and personal perspectives. Come join the excellence! **Overview** TNS Automation Development is looking for a passionate, motivated Platform Engineer to join our global team of Automation Developers and mature our devOps processes and automation infrastructure. The ideal candidate will have experience mapping, building, implementing, and maintaining platform management plans, great communication skills, and a passion for network automation. We’d prefer someone with 10\-15 years in IT who adds a wide set of skills to our own. ****Responsibilities**** **DevOps Automation Platform Engineer** An extraordinarily talented group of individuals work together every day to drive TNS' success, from both professional and personal perspectives. Come join the excellence! ****Overview**** TNS Automation Development is looking for a passionate, motivated Platform Engineer to join our global team of Automation Developers and mature our DevOps processes and automation infrastructure. The ideal candidate will have experience mapping, building, implementing, and maintaining platform management plans, great communication skills, and a passion for network automation. We’d prefer someone with 10\-15 years in IT who adds a wide set of skills to our own. **The Platform Engineer Will:** * Help us shape our DevOps Automation and AI journey; * Own our GitLab foundation including + Standards around repo creation, pipelines, and deployment. + Design, build, maintain, and optimize CI/CD pipelines. + Standardize and coordinate unit, integration, and security testing. + Plan, coordinate, publicize, and monitor application releases and updates. * Maintain the health and stability of our automation and orchestration applications, plugins, pipelines, and functions. * Define and document environment and infrastructure requirements, in particular around cloud, virtualized, and physical deployments. * Define and document patterns and policies for safe use and deployment of AI\-assisted automation, including but not limited to infrastructure, architecture, and deployment standards. * Work with Security and Risk teams to continuously assess our security posture. title: Electrical Maintenance Technician
description: **Background:** Veolia is Ireland’s leading environmental services company. We provide a comprehensive range of energy, waste and water solutions and are dedicated to carbon reduction, protecting the environment and building the circular economy. We offer all employees a benefits package as standard which includes full VHI cover, pension and education assistance. When you see the world as we do, you see the chance to help the world take better care of its resources, and help it become a better place for everyone. It's why we're looking for someone who's just as committed as we are, to push for genuine change and bring our ambition of Ecological Transformation to life. We know that everyone here at Veolia can help us work alongside our communities, look after the environment, and contribute to our inclusive culture. **Electrical Maintenance Technician** **Location: Blanchardstown** **Hybrid/Onsite: Onsite** **Working Pattern (Time/Hours): 39 Hours per week (07:30 to 16:00\)** **Duration: Permanent** **Overview of the role:** The Utilities Maintenance Technician is responsible for the execution of the preventive and corrective maintenance program as per client requirements in a safe, efficient and competent manner in line with cGMP and site protocols and procedures. He/she will work closely with day and shift colleagues and leads to ensure all work is carried out safely, correctly and as efficiently as possible. **Duties of the Role include;** * All work on site to be carried out in accordance with site EHS (Environmental Health and Safety) and Veolia EHS and cGMP rules and guidelines; any irregularities to be reported to the Maintenance \& Calibration Lead or Contract Manager. * Carry out work as per schedule supplied by the Maintenance \& Calibrations Lead, Planner and as per site policies, schedules and procedures. * Troubleshoot problems with utilities equipment as necessary and as directed by site supervisors * Complete site documentation before and after work tasks, as appropriate * Liaise with Contractors during planned service maintenance. * Participate in the appropriate training. * Carry out Risk Assessment of Equipment Maintenance Task List steps and ensure these are always kept valid. * Carry out Periodic reviews of WI’s/SOP to ensure documents are always current and valid. * Flexibility to work on equipment in all buildings * Work and interact with the client utilities engineering team * Flexibility to work around maintenance shutdown’s * Be familiar with all GMP rules and regulations * Be familiar with statutory requirements * Be responsible for his/her training curricula and ensure missing requirements are completed * Maintenance of housekeeping standards compatible with pharmaceutical manufacturing in the general plant, control rooms and compatibility with clean room requirements as required. Responsible for applying 5 S methodology in all areas. * Implement and participate in utilities reliability programs * If/when required, provide technical input/ support during the implementation of new projects from design through to beneficial use. **Required skills for this role;** * Senior Trades, City \& Guilds or equivalent in electrical engineering. * Electrical experience, 2 years minimum and good understanding of the basic fundamentals of the various utilities systems. ***Essential:*** * Experience on the following: + Pumps, HVAC Systems, Compressors, Boilers, steam systems and ancillary services, (DHW skids), Chillers, Cooling Towers, Clean steam Boilers, Purified Water Systems, CIP systems, Low /Medium Voltage Electrical Systems. * Good understanding of Preventive Maintenance Systems. * Work within a Computerized Maintenance Management Work Order system environment. * Work within a documented Permit to Work System * Computer literate * Ability to read and understand P\&ID’s. * Understanding of SOP’s and GMP Engineering systems. * Understanding of Basic Instrumentation * Must be able to demonstrate hands on experience with detailed knowledge/skill in the following area’s: * Team player. * Attention to detail * Methodical \& analytical approach to work. * Good problem solving and trouble shooting ability. * Good Initiative and pro\-active attitude in approach to duties ***Desirable:*** * Site Level 1 Electrical Qualification * Building Management Systems (Scada) * Experience of waste water treatment plants; * PLC’s * Troubleshooting / repair of mechanical systems **Please note:** If you are an EU/EEA national, you will be asked to show proof of right to work in Ireland. If you are a non\-EU/EEA national , you will require current and valid permission to work and reside in the Republic of Ireland. Information on legislation and guides to the procedures in relation to obtaining greencard permits ,work permits, spousal/dependant permits is available on the Department of Jobs, Enterprise and Innovation website.

title: Employee Experience Coordinator
description: **About Smartbox:** At Smartbox Group, all our energy and creativity are focused on providing the most beautiful gift to offer and to receive . **With nearly 15,000 different products** across leisure themes — Stays, Wellness, Adventure, Gastronomy — we allow people to enrich their lives with moments of sharing, emotions, and unforgettable memories. Present in **11 countries** , with **7 million experience gifts sold per year** and a business volume of nearly **€500 million** , Smartbox Group employs 700 **people across Europe** and works with over **41,000 trusted partners** (hotels, restaurants, wellness centres, outdoor activity providers…). Living more than owning, sharing more than having — this is more than ever a fundamental trend in our society, and it’s exactly what Smartbox offers. Do you want to inspire people to experience life? Join the pioneer and European leader of experience gifts! **The Role:** We’re looking for a **people\-loving, high\-energy Employee Experience Coordinator** to be the **friendly face of our Dublin office** and a key contributor to how our employees experience work every day. This role is ideal for a **graduate** (or early\-career professional) with a genuine **interest in HR, people operations, or workplace culture** , who thrives on variety, enjoys being around people, and isn’t afraid to grab a microphone or speak up in a room full of colleagues. You’ll sit at the heart of the office — blending **employee engagement, workplace experience, facilities coordination, and operational support** — helping to create an environment where people feel welcomed, supported, and excited to come into work. While your primary focus will be **Dublin** , you’ll also support other teams globally as needed. **Key Responsibilities:** **WORKPLACE EXPERIENCE \& EMPLOYEE ENGAGEMENT** **(What employees see, feel, and take part in)** * Champion a welcoming, energetic workplace experience that reflects Smartbox’s culture and values. * Design and deliver in‑office experiences, events, celebrations, and engagement initiatives that bring people together. * Partner with the People/HR team to support onboarding and offboarding experiences (badge access, welcome moments, swag). * Support delivery of the global employee engagement survey, including platform administration and basic insights support. * Use employee feedback to help identify improvements to workplace experience. * Own internal communication channels such as newsletters, intranet updates, and digital signage. * Administer the employee reward platform (Ben), including wallet funding checks and basic reporting. * Manage swag stock, ordering, and distribution across offices. **OFFICE OPERATIONS \& FACILITIES COORDINATION** **(How the office runs day to day)** * Act as the primary point of contact for office\-related queries and requests. * Oversee the smooth day‑to‑day running of the Dublin office, ensuring it is safe, organised, and well maintained. * Coordinate vendors including cleaning, maintenance, security, and office supplies. * Manage office\-related budgets, utilities, and invoices. * Support space planning, desk moves, and coordination with building management. * Ensure compliance with health \& safety requirements and company policies. * Maintain workplace documentation, processes, and best practices. * Support hybrid and remote working arrangements to ensure consistency of experience. **CULTURE, WELLBEING \& BELONGING** **(How it feels to work here)** * Act as a culture ambassador, helping Smartbox values show up in everyday office life. * Partner with the Social Committee to plan and deliver office events throughout the year, providing coordination and budget guidance. * Support wellbeing initiatives such as mental health awareness, ergonomic support, and fitness or wellness activities. * Assist with DEIB initiatives in partnership with the People Team, helping promote inclusion and belonging. * Help create an environment where all employees feel welcomed, supported, and able to be themselves at work. **OPERATIONS \& TEAM BACKUP** **(Critical but clearly contained)** * Provide backup support to other Operations team members, covering during annual leave, sick leave, or peak periods. * Support stationery ordering and general office administration for the Dublin office. * Coordinate health \& safety training requirements (e.g. fire wardens, first aiders). * Support People or Operations initiatives in other European offices when required, while remaining primarily Dublin\-focused. **Skills \& Experience:** * A third\-level degree is welcome — **HR is an advantage but not essential** * Strong communication and interpersonal skills * Comfortable using tools like Teams, Office 365, Jira (or keen to learn) * High attention to detail with a hospitality mindset * Comfortable using AI tools to work smarter * English is essential; a second European language is a bonus * Any experience in hospitality, events, admin, or customer\-facing roles is a plus * Background in hospitality, events, or employee engagement. (bonus!) **Why join us?** Because this role has **real impact** . You’ll shape how people feel at work, help create memorable moments, and be trusted as the **go\-to person** for the Dublin office experience. You’ll gain hands\-on exposure to: * HR \& People operations * Employee engagement \& wellbeing * Office and facilities management * Cross\-European collaboration And we’ll support your growth every step of the way. **About You:** We’re looking for someone who not only has the right skills but also embodies our guiding principles: **The Owner** – You take accountability, act with integrity, and treat challenges as opportunities. **The Ambitious Maverick** – You bring bold ideas, challenge the status quo, and are unafraid to take smart risks. **The Radical Optimist** – You maintain a solutions\-focused mindset, inspiring others with your positivity and resilience. **The Connector** – You build authentic relationships, foster collaboration, and create space for diverse voices to thrive. This role is much more about **attitude and potential** than years of experience. You might be a graduate, or in your first couple of roles — if you love people, we want to hear from you. You’re someone who is naturally **bubbly, approachable, and positive.** **What We Offer:** At Smartbox, we balance fairness, choice, and growth — supporting you in both work and life. * 🌍 **Transparency** : clear, fair salary bands and career levels * 📚 **Growth Mindset** : 4 Growth Days, Growth Leave \+ training budget * 🎁 **Flexible benefits** : personalize with health, wellbeing \& learning options * 🌈 **D\&I commitment** : Paid leave for parents, D\&I Day, gender equity in rewards * 🎉 **Unbox Your World** : YOLO day \+ paid sabbatical after 5 years * ✅ **Essentials** : health cover, pension, transport allowance, extra leave

Actively looking at ways to assist in the running of the business and provide support to actions that are related to assigned area of responsibility. * Providing expertise to Project Teams responsible for the introduction of new products and projects, ensuring successful implementation in the CDMO’s manufacturing area. * Collaborate with process and analytical teams to ensure accurate transfer of parameters, process understanding, and control strategies. * Effectively engaging and participating within a Lean culture environment where continuous improvement behaviours are expected, and programs are employed. The role will require incorporation of Lean concepts into assigned project(s). Managing and implementing projects that develop lean practices in the manufacturing areas. **Competencies/Skills:** * Ability to travel to sites in our CDMO network and ability to work successfully in a highly international environment * Problem solving skills: Gathers data from key stakeholders, analyses and interprets information to develop solutions to technical problems of moderate complexity * Project Management and organizational skills, including ability to follow assignments through to completion. * Demonstrated ability to work independently and deliver right first\-time results under minimal direction. * Escalate issues professionally and in a timely manner. * Negotiation and Influencing skills. * Take initiative to identify and drive improvements using lean six sigma tools. * Excellent verbal and written communication skills: Strong Technical writing skills for cGxP documentation (eg investigations, procedures, change controls) and good Presentation skills. * Decision Making skills and confidence to challenge others on decisions that impact the manufacturing area. * Teamwork and ability to coach, mentor and/or cross train colleagues within core technical areas. * Ensures compliance within regulatory environment. * Familiar with data analytics. **Education and Relevant Experience:** * Minimally Bachelor of Science, Engineering or technical discipline. * 5\+ years of manufacturing experience in relevant Drug Substance, Drug Product Manufacturing or related cGxP manufacturing experience. * Experience with regulatory compliance in cGxP manufacturing and in depth knowledge of Eudralex Volume 4, Annex 1 Manufacture of Sterile Medicinal Products.

title: HV Transmission Engineer
description: At Mace, our purpose is to redefine the boundaries of ambition. We believe in creating places that are responsible, bringing transformative impact to our people, communities, and societies across the globe. To learn more about our purpose, culture, and priorities, visit our strategy site. Within our consult business, we harness our unique combination of leading\-edge practical expertise and project delivery consultancy to unlock the potential in every project. The project: The role is within the technical design authority for the transmission system. Engineering and asset management is the area that facilitates that role through the development of technical standards and functional specifications for all plants to be connected to the transmission system and the development of new and innovative technologies and methodologies. In addition, the area is responsible for ensuring that requirements are incorporated in project design and construction whether the works are carried out by ESB networks or by third parties with medium voltage and high voltage projects. Our values shape the way we consult, and define the people we want to join us on our journey:* Safety first + Going home safe and well * Client focus \- Deliver on our promise * Integrity \- Always do the right thing * Create opportunity + for our people to excel You will be responsible for: * Ensuring that technical queries raised during the project are dealt with in accordance with TSO standards and policies and are closed out efficiently. * Ensuring that projects are carried out in a manner that meets requirements. * Reviewing and comparing submitted detailed designs against functional requirements and outline designs. * Monitoring of construction progress, and onsite inspection to ensure compliance with standards, functional requirements, and outline designs. * Ensuring developments are commissioned to comply with the requirements by producing and issuing commissioning instructions * Verifying transmission system requirements are met to allow commissioning tests to proceed. * Assisting the lead engineers with the engagement with customer and stakeholder technical consultants as and when required in order to resolve issues arising. * Coordinating with peers to meet business priorities, manage issues as they arise, provide technical guidance, and act as an escalation point to EAM manager. * Managing interactions on own projects with client engineering vendors, customers, and internal stakeholders. * Providing technical direction and assistance across the organisation on own projects and to team members and junior staff, and act as a team representative when required. * Ensuring the completion of stage gate reviews on all customer projects to ensure that the requirements of functional specifications are met. * Providing completion and dissemination of lessons learned on each project post project energisation. * Ensuring appropriate engagement with our partners in ESB networks including meetings, reporting, relationship management, and general communication as applicable to the client engineering role. * Facilitating and inputting project reviews, assessment, and auditing by internal and external parties as required. You’ll need to have: * You have an electrical engineering degree or equivalent with suitable experience within MV/HV projects. * You have transmission substation design expertise with experience assessing substation designs for conformity to standards at 110/220/400 kV (MV, HV). * You are digitally savvy, can build lasting client relationships, and lead strong, motivated teams. * You will have a construction or project management background and be hungry to grow your career and enjoy the opportunities Mace has to offer. You will have experience completing onsite inspections and monitoring construction progress for compliance to ensure transmission assets can be commissioned in accordance with standards. * Experience preparing and issuing transmission design drawings, such as single line diagrams, substation layout drawings, protection schematics and interlocking drawings. You’ll also have: * Membership of RICS, CIOB, APM, ICE or equivalent demonstrable professional or personal development. * Substantial experience in the successful delivery phase of projects and programmes in the energy sector. * Property or infrastructure project management expertise. * Commercial and financial acumen. * Experience of leading parts of projects within large, diverse teams. * Experience of developing and forming relationships with senior stakeholders.Knowledge of how carbon and sustainability considerations will shape the outlook of the projects you are working on. Mace is an inclusive employer and welcomes interest from a diverse range of candidates. Even if you feel you do not fulfil all of the criteria, please apply as you may still be the best candidate for this role or another role within our organization. \#LI\-Hybrid

title: Human Resources Business Partner
description: **About Chartered Accountants Ireland** Chartered Accountants Ireland is Ireland’s leading professional accountancy body, representing 40,000 members in over 100 countries and educating 8,600 students. Its members provide leadership across both the public and private sector, bringing experience, trusted expertise, and strict standards to all aspects of their work. As the largest professional body on the island of Ireland, Chartered Accountants Ireland engages with several stakeholders including governments, policy makers, regulators, and business groups on key issues affecting the profession and the wider economy. Chartered Accountants Ireland supports members at every stage of their career from education to qualification to continuing professional development. **Institute Values** The Institute strives to embed an environment based on our Values of **Excellence, Forward\-Looking, Openness and Accountability** . Candidates should be able to demonstrate how these may apply to them in the role for which they are applying and how their previous experience is relevant to them. **About The Role** We are recruiting an experienced HR Business Partner to join our team. Working collaboratively with HR colleagues, the successful candidate will provide strategic and operational HR support to designated business functions across areas including organisational design, change management, employee engagement and employee relations. The ideal candidate will be a strong team player and will demonstrate a high level of trust and flexibility while possessing a broad understanding of all major HR disciplines This role will report to the Director of Human Resources and Organisational Development. **Business Partnering** * Develop strong professional relationships with the business and proactively work with key stakeholders to deliver solutions\-focused HR advice consistent within the HR framework. * Coach and support senior managers on leadership capabilities including change management, people management and leadership development. * Contribute to the development and implementation of business plans and strategies. **Strategic Project \& Continuous Improvement** * Manage or support the delivery of specific business focused strategic HR projects and undertake specific project work as requested. * Identify opportunities to improve people related processes and ways of working in line with the HR strategy. * Partner with managers to drive high levels of employee engagement by implementing actionable improvements from employee engagement survey results. * Support the roll\-out of diversity and well\-being initiatives as part of the HR team to nurture an inclusive organisational culture. * Support effective internal communication strategies using a variety of channels and forums. **Employee Engagement** * Partner with managers to drive high levels of employee engagement by implementing actionable improvements from employee engagement survey results. * Support the roll\-out of diversity and well\-being initiatives as part of the HR team to nurture an inclusive organisational culture. * Support effective internal communication strategies using a variety of channels and forums. **Change Management** * Collaborate with leaders and stakeholders to design and deliver effective change management plans supporting organisational design and broader change initiatives. * Provide guidance and support to managers and employees through periods of organisational change. * Drive a culture of openness by communicating clearly with colleagues and engaging with any queries with a solutions focused mindset. **Employee Relations** * Provide expert employee relations advice, management and guidance to the stakeholder group. * Manage disciplinary matters, grievances and any other performance related cases as they arise. * Facilitate talent management and succession planning discussions within business areas. * Identify and source appropriate development interventions in line with the L\&D budget and recommended training vendors. * Maintain up‑to‑date knowledge of legal developments to mitigate risk and support compliant decision‑making across both RoI and NI jurisdictions. **Talent Management \& Succession Planning** * Facilitate talent management and succession planning discussions within business areas. * Identify and source appropriate development interventions in line with the L\&D budget and recommended training vendors. **Additional responsibilities:** * Contribute to budget management and identify opportunities for cost efficiencies. * Participate in cross‑functional and corporate projects as required. * Undertake additional duties relevant to the role as organisational needs evolve. **The Person:** **Qualification and Experience** * A broad understanding of all major HR disciplines with a minimum of 4\-5 years’ experience within a HR Business Partnering role with strong generalist and operational experience. * Demonstrated ability to build effective relationships with key stakeholders. * Proven experience supporting organizational change. * Strong business and financial acumen with attention to detail. * Demonstrate critical, analytical and innovative thinking skills. * Degree level qualification with a professional qualification in HR e.g. CIPD Graduate Diploma/Certificate in HR desirable **Personal Attributes** * Self\-motivated, adaptable and collaborative team member. * Strong organisation and time management skills. * Excellent verbal and written communication skills, with an ability to influence professionally at all levels within the organisation.

title: ICU/Anaesthetic Registrar
description: ### **Job Description** **Job Title:** **ICU/Anaesthetic Registrar** **Department:** **Anaesthesia and Intensive Care** **Reports to: Clinically and professionally responsible to the Director of Intensive Care or Daily Duty Intensivist. /Administratively accountable to the Head of the NCHD Office** **Date: 2026** **Overall Purpose of Job** -------------------------- The mission of Beacon Hospital is to provide quality care to all patients, in a respectful, safe and compassionate environment. In exercising this mission, Beacon Hospital will adhere to all health and safety regulations. To provide care to patients in the Operating Theatre and Critical Care Units, and to any other patients in the Hospital who require acute medical intervention that can be provided by an Anaesthetic Registrar, which includes, but is not limited to: Emergency situations including airway management. Daily review of all the patients in the Intensive Care Unit. Participation in the Intensive Care Unit Ward Rounds. Transfer if necessary patients for ICU admission from theatre. Follow up of post\-operative surgical patients. Acute decompensation of Medical patients. Any patients with fluid management issues including difficult vascular access. Cardiopulmonary resuscitation. Acute pain management. For assistance in theatre as required under consultant supervision. The Anaesthesia and Intensive Care Medicine Department at Beacon Hospital is a consultant delivered service. The team consists of the Anaesthetic Consultant who works in collaboration with Anaesthetic Registrars in patient care and in formulating management plans for patients. **Key Responsibilities and Deliverables** ----------------------------------------- To act under the supervision of the Daily Duty Intensivist/Anaesthetist in the provision of an Intensive Care service and to support the Daily Duty Intensivist in their duties. At 07:00 and /or 19:00 go to the Critical Care Unit and liaise with the Nurse Manager or Nurse in Charge. Take hand over of patients from previous shift Registrar, so that continuity of care is ensured and immediate decisions are made and orders written. To review patients in the Critical Care Unit on a regular basis in accordance with the plan of care made at the beginning of the shift and inform the consultant of any issues or problems. Document review findings in clinical notes in a timely and legible manner. To attend to emergencies when they occur or free up the consultant to attend to emergencies. To respond to any adverse occurrences e.g. injuries, falls, sudden illnesses, etc suffered by patients, visitors or staff. To contribute to quality improvement; risk management initiatives and policy development so that the overall Anaesthesia Service is of the highest quality and reflective of best practice. To assist in the education of Critical Care colleagues and staff. To participate in work related projects at Beacon Hospital. To act and behave professionally and perform one's duties in a manner that optimises patient care and protects and enhances the reputation of Beacon Hospital. To adhere to Hospital Policies and Procedures e.g. JCI, Code Blue, infection control, work hours and overtime. To comply with Health and Safety Regulations, ensuring safe working practices in a safe working environment. To undertake any other duties that may be required, which could include assisting medical colleagues throughout the hospital and acting as a resource for more junior colleagues. **Person Specification** **Qualifications:** * MCAI, FCAI, MRCP or MRCS equivalent is required. * Current Full Irish Medical Council Registration. * Membership of the College of Anaesthetists is desirable. **Experience:** * At least 2 years' experience as an Anaesthetic Registrar in an acute hospital setting. **Job Specific Competencies and Knowledge:** * Current ACLS certification required. * Current PALS certification required. **Personal Competencies:** * Proven ability to work as an effective team member. * Ability to communicate effectively. * All posts in Beacon Hospital require a high level of flexibility to ensure the delivery of an effective and efficient service. Therefore, the post holder will be required to demonstrate flexibility as and when required by their manager or Hospital management. **This job description is intended to be an outline of the areas of responsibility and deliverables at the time of its writing. As the Hospital and the post holder develop, this job description may be subject to review in light of the changing needs of the Hospital.**

title: IPG Reporting Analyst
description: Investigator Payments Reporting Analyst \- Dublin \- Office Hybrid ICON plc is a world\-leading healthcare intelligence and clinical research organization. We’re proud to foster an inclusive environment driving innovation and excellence, and we welcome you to join us on our mission to shape the future of clinical development. **The Role** The Investigator Payments Group (IPG) is the investigator payments function for ICON Clinical Research. The department role is to ensure timely, accurate and efficient execution of investigator payments \& to ensure all payments are carried out in accordance with ICON policies and procedures. **The Analyst Role Is To** * Support reporting requirements ensuring compliance with any Healthcare reporting legislation * Support operational reporting for IPG team **Role Responsibilities Include** * Recognise, exemplify and adhere to ICON's values which centre around our commitment to People, Clients and Performance. * Complete reports and tracking in a timely and consistent manner with concise, accurate information in various systems and tracking tools. * Ensure proper documentation and record tracking of meetings, decisions, interventions and escalations. * Liaise with customers to determine report requirements. * Interrogate \& analyse data and prepare relevant metrics. Accurate analysis with limited supervision is expected. * Ability to work under pressure and meet tight deadlines, where necessary * Become proficient in systems, tools and ICON’s applications to efficiently and accurately analyse data. * Perform other reasonably related duties, analysis and special projects as assigned by management team. * As a member of staff, the employee is expected to embrace and contribute to our culture of process improvement with a focus on streamlining our processes adding value to our business and meeting client needs. **To be successful in the role, you will ideally have:** * Bachelor’s degree in finance, data analytics, business administration, or a related field * Prior relevant experience related to data analysis and production of reports is essential * Advanced excel skills are essential (Pivot tables, lookups, etc.) * Demonstrate a logical approach to analysis with a questioning attitude * Numerate with some financial understanding * Excellent working knowledge of MS office package * Excellent communication (written and oral), organisational and customer service skills are essential * Self\-starter who can work with minimal supervision **What ICON Can Offer You** Our success depends on the quality of our people. That’s why we’ve made it a priority to build a diverse culture that rewards high performance and nurtures talent. In addition to your competitive salary, ICON offers a range of additional benefits. Our benefits are designed to be competitive within each country and are focused on well\-being and work life balance opportunities for you and your family. **Our Benefits Examples Include** * Various annual leave entitlements * A range of health insurance offerings to suit you and your family’s needs. * Competitive retirement planning offerings to maximize savings and plan with confidence for the years ahead. * Global Employee Assistance Programme, LifeWorks, offering 24\-hour access to a global network of over 80,000 independent specialized professionals who are there to support you and your family’s well\-being. * Life assurance * Flexible country\-specific optional benefits, including childcare vouchers, bike purchase schemes, discounted gym memberships, subsidized travel passes, health assessments, among others. Visit our careers site to read more about the benefits ICON offers. At ICON, inclusion \& belonging are fundamental to our culture and values. We’re dedicated to providing an inclusive and accessible environment for all candidates. ICON is committed to providing a workplace free of discrimination and harassment. All qualified applicants will receive equal consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability or protected veteran status. If, because of a medical condition or disability, you need a reasonable accommodation for any part of the application process, or in order to perform the essential functions of a position, please let us know or submit a request here Interested in the role, but unsure if you meet all of the requirements? We would encourage you to apply regardless – there’s every chance you’re exactly what we’re looking for here at ICON whether it is for this or other roles. Are you a current ICON Employee? Please click here to apply

title: IRE_QA Warehouse Specialist
description: Life changing therapies. Global impact.
