title: Junior UX/UI designer
description: At Bending Spoons, we’re striving to build one of the all\-time great companies. A company that serves a huge number of customers. A company where team members grow to their full potential. A company that functions at unparalleled levels of effectiveness and efficiency. A company that creates value for shareowners at an extraordinary rate. And a company that does so while adhering to high ethical standards. In pursuit of this objective, we acquire and improve digital businesses, not to sell on, but to own and operate for the long term. The transformations we make are often deep—designed to speed up innovation, benefit customers, and strengthen business performance. Here, hierarchy is minimal and teams are small and talent\-dense. We operate established products with the ambition, agility, and urgency of a startup. Across the company, we integrate AI deeply into how we work so that human judgment and machine intelligence reinforce each other. For a talented, driven, and collaborative individual, working at Bending Spoons is an opportunity to learn, make an impact, and progress their career at an exceptionally high rate. That’s our promise to such a candidate. **A few examples of your responsibilities** * **Shape high\-leverage product experiences.** Design exceptional UI and UX—from early concepts to polished delivery—using the best available tools to explore more directions, move faster, and raise the quality bar. Ensure every experience is intuitive, effective, and delightful. * **Start with an LLM.** Use AI tools to clarify intent, draft short specs, and surface risks, edge cases, and initial approaches before opening any design tool. Use this work to align quickly with your team and compress the time from ambiguity to direction. * **Prototype and build with AI.** Use Cursor and Claude Code to turn rough concepts into interactive prototypes and generate production\-ready frontend code. You know when to reach for code and when to reach for Figma—and you move fluidly between both to get the best result. * **Co\-create with PMs and engineers from day one.** Define UX requirements early, stay in the build through QA and release, and make engineering\-aware design decisions. * **Validate and measure.** Talk directly with customers, run quick tests, and use what you learn to adjust direction. Track funnels, retention, and adoption to assess design effectiveness and sharpen what ships next. * **Find the highest\-leverage bets from the backlog.** Identify where product improvements can unlock business value, align stakeholders around them, and move fast to deliver. **What we look for** * **Reasoning ability.** Given the necessary knowledge, you can solve complex problems. You think from first principles, and structure your ideas sharply. You resist the influence of biases. You identify and take care of the details that matter. * **Drive.** You’re extremely ambitious in everything you do—and your initiative, effort, and tenacity match the intensity of your ambition. You feel deeply responsible for your work. You hold yourself to a high—and rising—bar. * **Team spirit.** You give generously and without the expectation of receiving in return. You support the best idea, not your idea. You're always happy to get your hands dirty to help your team. You’re reliable, honest, and transparent. * **Proficiency in English.** You read, write, and speak proficiently in English. **What we offer** * **Incredibly talented, entrepreneurial teams.** You’ll work in small, result\-oriented, autonomous teams alongside some of the brightest people in your field. * **An exceptional opportunity for growth.** We go to great lengths to hire individuals of outstanding potential—then, our priority is to put them in the ideal position to thrive. Spooners in their 20s lead products worth hundreds of millions of dollars. And if you’ve got what it takes, you’ll soon be playing an essential role in major projects, too. * **Competitive pay and access to equity in the company.** Typically, we offer individuals at the start of their career an annual salary of £85,797 in London and €66,065 elsewhere in Europe. For a candidate that we assess as possessing considerable relevant experience, the salary on offer tends to be between £112,189 and £250,512 in London, and €107,837 and €188,848 elsewhere in Europe. Compensation varies by location and expected impact, and grows rapidly as you gain experience and translate it into greater contributions. For individuals who demonstrate exceptional capability, we may offer compensation that extends beyond the usual ranges to reflect their higher expected impact. If you're offered a permanent contract, you'll also be able receive some of your pay in company equity at a discounted price, thus participating in the value creation we achieve together. If relocating to Italy, you may enjoy a 50% tax cut. * **All. These. Benefits.** Flexible hours, remote working, unlimited backing for learning and training, top\-of\-the\-market health insurance, a rich relocation package, generous parental support, and a yearly retreat to a stunning location. We help each Spooner set up the conditions to do their best work. **Commitment \& contract** Permanent or fixed\-term. Full\-time. **Location** Milan (Italy), London (UK), Madrid (Spain), Warsaw (Poland) or remote in selected countries. **The selection process** In our screening process, we prioritize verifiable signals of excellence, regardless of seniority. Some people hold back because they feel they lack experience or have an “imperfect” CV. If you like the role and believe you could excel over time, don’t self\-reject. If you pass our screening, you’ll be asked to complete one or more tests. They are challenging, may involve unfamiliar problems, and can take several hours. We set the bar high and won’t extend an offer until we’re confident we’ve found the right candidate. This is why a job may remain open for months or be reposted several times. We consider all applicants for employment and provide reasonable accommodations for individuals with disabilities—please let us know through this form. **Before you apply** If you’ve applied before but didn't receive an offer, we recommend waiting at least one year before applying again. Bending Spoons is a demanding environment. We’re extremely ambitious and we hold ourselves—and one another—to a high standard. While this tends to lead to extraordinary learning, achievement, and career growth, it also requires significant commitment. To help you ramp up quickly and set yourself up for success, we recommend spending your first few months working from our Milan office, regardless of your long\-term work location. It’s the best way to rapidly absorb our company culture and build trust with your new teammates. We’ll support you with generous travel and accommodation assistance. After that, you’re welcome to work from our offices in Milan or London, or remotely from approved countries—depending on what we agree at the offer stage. If the role speaks to you and you’re excited to give your best, we’d love to hear from you. Apply now—we can’t wait to meet you.

title: Legal PA, Litigation & Dispute Management - Dublin
description: We are Eversheds Sutherland, a global law firm, with more than 5,000 people across over 30 countries. We’re full\-service with deep niche and sector experience. Whatever challenge, wherever in the world, we’re equipped and ready to meet it. We live our values, we’re purposeful and purpose\-led. So although the world is fast\-moving and rapidly changing, we see it as a place where everyone can thrive. We’re ambitious for our clients, our communities – and for you. Whether you’re starting out on your career or well established, whether you’re a lawyer or in business services. If you’re looking for what’s next, we are too. **What To Expect** As a Legal PA within our Practice Group Support team, your role is pivotal. You’ll provide confidential, professional and proactive support to a designated group of stakeholders. Confidence, effective communication and influential skills are essential. We value an assertive and forward\-thinking approach. Here’s what to expect: **Diverse responsibilities:** Our Legal PAs handle a wide range of tasks, supporting various aspects of our business. Empowerment and mentorship: You’ll lead by example, fostering ongoing personal and professional development among junior colleagues and peers. **Continuous learning:** We invest in your professional development through our development plans. We offer personalised mentoring and coaching, technical training and soft skills courses to boost motivation and success in your career path. **Culture of feedback:** Our culture thrives on continuous feedback that builds confidence, development and recognition of achievements. **Pro Bono, Community and Diversity \& Inclusion (D\&I) opportunities:** Beyond your daily tasks, we encourage you to contribute to Pro Bono, Community and D\&I initiatives. Make a difference by using your skills to benefit our communities. **Ambition for people, clients and communities:** Our ambition extends beyond business success. We are equally committed to the growth and well\-being of our people, clients and the broader community. **Staying ahead:** Collaborate closely with lawyers, take ownership of tasks and exceed business expectations, anticipate challenges, adapt to changing technologies. in the legal sector. For talented, ambitious individuals there's no better place to be working in the law industry. **Role And Key Responsibilities** **Stakeholder engagement:** Conduct regular one to one meetings with stakeholders to discuss actions, priorities, expectations and future planning. Attend team meetings to stay informed and contribute effectively. Act as a liaison point for both internal and external clients, draft clear and concise replies to incoming correspondence. Assist with client queries during stakeholder absences. Maintain a flexible approach and adopt hybrid working to accommodate stakeholder and client needs. **Adaptability and collaboration:** Demonstrate client service excellence by being responsive to stakeholder requests. Support the wider PA team, covering for team members during absences or when required to ensure continued resource. **Efficient mailbox, diary and travel management:** Proactively manage mailboxes and diaries including travel coordination. Anticipate future needs and take initiative to resolve conflicts. **Meeting organisation and coordination:** Draft agendas and prepare for internal and external meetings. Ensure necessary equipment and refreshments for both physical and virtual meetings using available technology and resources. **Workflow management and delegation:** Own the end\-to\-end process, be accountable for successful conclusion of all stakeholder requests. Delegate tasks effectively across dedicated support teams such as billing, finance, team assistants, document production to meet expectations and deadlines in line with the Scope of Service. **Document management:** Undertake urgent and short document work. Collaborate with the Document Production Team for larger documents utilising our dedicated software. **Matter management:** Manage the entire client and matter opening process including electronic filing, collaborating with stakeholders and other business support teams. **Financial administration and compliance:** Provide financial administrative support to stakeholders, coordinating with billing and finance teams, ensuring compliance with legal, firm\-wide and practice\-specific policies and procedures. **Pitch and presentation creation:** Collaborate with the document production team to create impactful pitches and PowerPoint presentations for stakeholders. **Marketing and business development:** Coordinate and organise events, create event material, digital working lists and communicate with attendees to ensure a seamless end to end process. **Championing process improvements:** Be an advocate for process changes within the organisation, influence decisions and be a part of the solution. **Efficient administration:** Manage electronic filing, records maintenance and expenses effectively using the firm's designated software. **Technology utilisation:** Leverage the firm’s technology as a key business tool for managing, updating, reporting and research. Create and maintain data sites for efficient collaboration. Seek opportunities to embrace technologies that improve our ways of working and drive productivity in day\-to\-day activities. **Skills And Experience** We are looking for: * Experience in a similar environment: Experience working within a comparable professional setting, providing seamless adaptation to our busy environment. * Client service excellence: Strive to exceed client expectations by delivering outstanding service. Anticipate client needs and proactively address any issues. * Problem\-solving and initiative: Be a proactive problem solver. Anticipate challenges, take initiative and implement solutions efficiently. Aspire for excellence in all tasks. Show dedication and commitment to achieving strategic goals. * Effective communication: Demonstrate excellent communication skills when liaising with stakeholders and clients. Your ability to convey information clearly and professionally is essential. * Delegation and ownership: Delegate tasks across dedicated support teams while maintaining overall ownership of transactional activities. * High\-level support: Provide exceptional PA support to senior stakeholders, including managing calendars, organising meetings and handling confidential information. * Relationship building: Cultivate strong relationships both internally and externally. Collaborate effectively with colleagues and clients. * Task management and risk awareness: Skilfully manage tasks with expertise, seeking ways to enhance efficiency and quality. Balance priorities and stay alert to risks, ensuring tasks are completed on time and stakeholder expectations are met. * Flexibility: Demonstrate flexibility and embrace change. Adapt seamlessly to evolving work environments. * Microsoft applications proficiency: Demonstrate intermediate to advanced skills in Microsoft applications including Outlook, Teams and Copilot. * Team collaboration: Contribute to a positive team environment. title: Maintenance Engineer
description: **Electrical Maintenance Engineer (Shift\-Based \| 4\-Day Work Week)** **Be part of something being built, not just maintained.** This is a great opportunity to join high\-volume manufacturing operation entering an exciting phase of growth and investment. As part of this, a **new Electrical Maintenance Engineer role** has been created. If you’re someone who enjoys solving problems, improving systems, and being trusted to make an impact, this is a role where you won’t just “keep things running”… you’ll help define how they run in the future. **What You’ll Be Doing** * Diagnosing and resolving faults across production and site equipment * Supporting the reliability of automated machinery (e.g. conveyors, packaging systems, processing equipment) * Carrying out planned preventative maintenance and condition monitoring * Responding quickly to breakdowns to minimise downtime * Contributing to continuous improvement and reliability initiatives * Performing root cause analysis and implementing long\-term fixes * Maintaining accurate maintenance records and system updates * Working collaboratively with production and leadership teams * **Playing a key role in the installation, testing, and optimisation of new equipment** **What We’re Looking For** * Qualified in an electrical discipline (time\-served Craft Electrician or equivalent, ) * At least 2 years’ experience in a similar industrial or manufacturing environment * Strong fault\-finding and problem\-solving ability * Comfortable working hands\-on with both electrical and mechanical systems * A proactive mindset you don’t wait to be told, you take ownership * Flexible and team\-oriented approach to shift work * Good communication skills and attention to detail **What’s On Offer** * **€70,000 \+ DOE** * Pension Contribution 5% * 40\-hour week across a 4\-day shift pattern * Annual medical * Full PPE and your own professional toolbox provided **Working Hours \& Shift Pattern** * 4\-day working week (4 shifts over 7 days) * 4 x 10 hour shifts \-3 days off back to back * Start times rotate between: * 4:00am – 2:00pm * 6:00am – 4:00pm * 12:00pm – 10:00pm **Why Apply?** This isn’t a backfill role, you’ll be joining at a time of **real investment and change** . If you want to move beyond reactive maintenance and **get exposure to projects, installs, and improvements** , this is the right opportunity.

title: Maintenance Support Engineer
description: **Maintenance Support Engineer – Syringe Filling Operations** **Position Summary** The Maintenance Department Support Engineer is responsible for day\-to\-day commercial support within the maintenance department. The role will span numerous areas such as, but not limited too, shutdown coordination, engineering reliability projects, supporting and leading all quality non\-conformances and leading maintenance equipment RCA’s, leading and coordinating all training\-related activities within the maintenance department of a pharmaceutical syringe filling plant. There will also be a training aspect of this role which ensures that maintenance personnel are effectively onboarded, trained, and continuously developed to support safe, compliant, and efficient manufacturing operations. The position also plays a key role in building and sustaining a high\-performing training hub aligned with site training academy initiatives. **Key Responsibilities** * Support Maintenance Reliability and Shutdown Planning * The Maintenance support engineer is responsible for improving the reliability, availability, and maintainability of plant equipment and utilities within the ADL manufacturing facility. The role focuses on leading and developing shutdown planning and sustaining a robust reliability framework that ensures right\-first\-time maintenance execution, minimises unplanned downtime, and optimises asset lifecycle performance in support of safe, compliant, and efficient operations. * The role partners closely with Maintenance, Engineering, Operations, and Quality to analyse equipment performance, identify failure modes, and implement preventive and predictive maintenance strategies. The Maintenance Reliability Engineer also leads continuous improvement initiatives to reduce non\-commercial time, improve equipment uptime, and increase overall plant availability. * Hiring \& Onboarding Coordination (Maintenance Department) * Coordinate all hiring logistics for maintenance technicians in partnership with HR and maintenance leadership. * Lead onboarding processes, ensuring a structured and compliant induction for all new hires. * Serve as the primary point of contact for new maintenance employees during onboarding. * Ensure all onboarding documentation, training assignments, and compliance requirements are completed on time. * Training Program Development (Syringe Filling Lines) * Design, develop, and implement technical training programs for new and existing syringe filling lines. * Collaborate with SMEs (Subject Matter Experts), engineering, and operations to ensure training content reflects current equipment, processes, and regulatory requirements. * Standardize training materials, work instructions, and qualification processes across all syringe lines. * Incorporate safety, GMP, and operational excellence principles into all training curricula. * Maintenance Technician Training \& Onboarding Program * Develop, implement, and continuously improve a structured onboarding and qualification program for new maintenance technicians. * Define competency frameworks, training pathways, and certification requirements. * Monitor trainee progress and provide feedback to supervisors and stakeholders. * Identify skill gaps and implement targeted development plans for both new and existing technicians. **Qualifications \& Experience:** * Bachelor’s degree in Engineering (Mechanical, Electrical or Chemical) * Manufacturing experience in biopharmaceutical Formulation/Fill/Finish or Active Pharmaceutical Ingredient (API) facilities * Solid leadership, technical writing, and communication/presentation skills * Strong understanding of GMP, regulatory requirements, and training compliance standards. * Proven experience in training program development, technical training, or learning \& development. * Experience working with maintenance teams, equipment, and troubleshooting processes is highly desirable. **Skills \& Competencies:** * Excellent strategic planning and demonstrated project management skills * Strong organizational and coordination skills with the ability to manage multiple priorities. * Excellent communication and stakeholder management abilities. * Instructional design and training delivery expertise. * Analytical mindset with the ability to track and improve training effectiveness. * Leadership capability with a focus on coaching and developing others. * High attention to detail and commitment to quality and compliance. **Key Performance Indicators (KPIs)** * Time\-to\-competency for new maintenance hires * Training completion and compliance rates * Effectiveness of training programs (assessment results, performance improvements) * Reduction in maintenance\-related deviations linked to skill gaps * Continuous improvement of training materials and programs **Reporting Structure** * Reports to: Maintenance Manager / Site Training Lead (as applicable) * Key Interfaces: HR, Engineering, Operations, Quality, Training Academy

title: Manufacturing Bioprocess Associate
description: **Job Description** An amazing opportunity has arisen for a **Manufacturing Bioprocess Associate** , in Dunboyne. In this shift role (12/7\), you will provide operational support for shop floor operations of the Late Stage and Launch Pipeline, at our new state of the art single use biotechnology facility. At our company, we aspire to be the premier research\-intensive biopharmaceutical company. Our Biologics facility in Dunboyne is a state\-of\-the\-art facility that integrates leading\-edge technology with a dynamic workspace to support seamless connection and collaboration. BioNX provides Biologics Drug Substance manufacturing for Clinical Supply, Registration \& Commercial Launch, operating in a multi\-product mode to adapt to changing demands. **What You Will Do** * Support end\-to\-end production operations for both Fed Batch and Continuous Manufacturing. * Operate equipment according to electronic batch records, sampling plans, and standard operating procedures. * Support weekend or out\-of\-hours work on select time frames to support manufacturing activities. * Conduct all work activities with strict adherence to the safety and compliance culture on site. * Support the authoring of electronic batch records, sampling plans, work instructions, and standard operating procedures. * Work as part of a multi\-disciplined, diverse, and dedicated process team where flexibility and teamwork are essential requirements. **What Skills You Will Need** In order to excel in this role, you will more than likely have: **Required** * Level 6 qualification in a science or engineering discipline. * 2\+ years of experience in a GMP Manufacturing environment. * Ability to work independently and within a cross\-functional team. * Familiarity with contamination control and batch release requirements. * Understanding of Upstream and Downstream Unit Operations for mAb manufacturing. **Preferred** * Familiarity with Emerson DeltaV, Pas X, and the use of Automation in a manufacturing process. * Experience with various SingleUse technologies in a manufacturing environment. * Knowledge in cell expansion and or, cell banking operations. **Required Skills** Adaptability, Adaptability, Analytical Thinking, Applied Engineering, Automation, Cleanroom Gowning, Cognitive Flexibility, Computer Literacy, Contamination Control, Equipment Set title: Market Operations
description: A challenging role within a fast\-growing operations environment where the candidate will have the opportunity to expand his/her knowledge of the different products offered within the Securities Business. The role will require the candidate to assume responsibility for day\-to\-day processing of the full Domestic Markets teams processes. This will involve acting as a first point of contact for any queries related to the teams’ processes. **Responsibilities** : * A junior member on the Domestic markets team * Demonstrate ability to manage internal and external client relationships, to ensure the department responsibilities and objectives are met. * Coordinate and where necessary assist with any clerical tasks/activities within area depending on staffing levels and volumes. * Complete daily checklist ensuring all tasks have been signed as completed. * Daily monitoring of tasks to ensure all deliverables are completed in accordance with their corresponding cut off times * Act as first point of contact within the team \- liaise with other teams and clients to resolve escalated problems * Complete weekly MIS reporting with justifications for volumes, aged items and fluctuations. * Ensure Department Audit Requirements and procedures are adhered to * Ensure the completion of all UAT for system enhancements * Promote innovation within the team by looking at ways to streamline and improve current processes by technical enhancement or strategic positioning of functions within the group **Qualifications** : * Excellent verbal and written communication skills and strong interpersonal skills * Excellent Client service skills * Excellent attention to detail * Strong organisational skills \& ability to work accurately under pressure meeting strict daily deadlines * Maintain high work rates irrespective of fluctuating volume * Ideally from an Operations background with a sound understanding of financial securities * Agency \& Trust /Corporate Trust Operations advantageous

title: Mechanical Estimator
description: **Senior Mechanical Estimator** My client is a leading engineering and construction specialist delivering complex, large\-scale projects across Europe, with particular expertise in mission\-critical environments such as data centres. My Client provide integrated mechanical and electrical solutions from design through commissioning. Due to continued growth, my client is seeking an experienced **Senior Mechanical Estimator** to join its Dublin\-based pre\-construction team. **Role Summary** The Senior Mechanical Estimator will be responsible for preparing accurate, competitive, and strategic cost estimates for mechanical packages on high\-value projects. This position plays a key role in securing new work and supporting project success from the tender stage through to contract award. **Key Responsibilities** * Lead the preparation of **detailed mechanical cost estimates** across a range of large\-scale projects * Analyse **drawings, specifications, and tender documentation** to define the scope and identify risks * Manage the **full tender process** , including subcontractor enquiries and bid submissions * Source and evaluate **supplier and subcontractor pricing** * Conduct **value engineering** and cost optimisation exercises * Support **bid reviews and commercial adjudications** with senior stakeholders * Collaborate with design, commercial, and delivery teams to ensure alignment * Mentor junior team members and contribute to the development of the estimating function **Candidate Profile** * Degree\-qualified in Mechanical Engineering, Building Services, or a related discipline * **7–10\+ years’ experience** in mechanical estimating within an M\&E or construction environment * Strong experience working on **large\-scale or mission\-critical projects** * Solid technical knowledge of mechanical systems, including HVAC and pipework * Commercially astute with strong analytical and negotiation skills * Proficient in estimating tools and Excel * Excellent communication and stakeholder management abilities **Salary \& Benefits Package** * **Base Salary:** €90,000 – €110,000 * **Bonus:** Discretionary performance\-related bonus * **Annual Leave:** 25 days \+ public holidays * **Car Allowance:** €6,000 – €8,000 per annum * **Pension:** 7\.5% employer contribution * **Private Healthcare:** Included * **Life Assurance \& Income Protection** * **Professional Development:** Training and career progression opportunities * **Project Exposure:** Opportunity to work on major European projects within a high\-growth sector

title: Mobile Mechanic
description: **Mobile Technician – Full Time** Dublin (Field\-Based) United Tyres Autoservice Job Type: Full\-time, Permanent **Purpose of the Role** Carry out mobile jobs safely and professionally, represent United Tyres Autoservice to a high standard in the field, and make good decisions on when work should be escalated to the workshop or recovery. **Why This Role Matters** Mobile work is often the **first real experience customers have with United Tyres Autoservice**. In this growth phase, the role is critical. Done well, it builds trust, protects vehicle uptime, and ensures the right work flows back into the workshop properly. **Key Responsibilities** * Attend mobile jobs **on time** and complete in\-scope work safely and efficiently * Carry out repairs neatly and to a **high standard** * Capture clear **proof of work**, including photos and notes where required * Maintain **van cleanliness**, stock awareness, and proper equipment care * Communicate clearly with the **Senior Mobile Technician and front desk** when jobs change, fail, or need escalation * Represent United Tyres Autoservice professionally with **customers, drivers, and fleet contacts** **What Success Looks Like** * Strong punctuality and **safe, consistent workmanship** * Clear and professional **job completion and handover discipline** * Smooth hand\-offs when jobs require **workshop support, recovery, or parts** * A well\-maintained van and **reliable field performance** **What We’re Looking For** * Confident carrying out **on\-site repairs** (brakes, discs, batteries, punctures, basic mechanical work) * Good judgment \- knows when a roadside fix should **not be pushed too far** * Strong work ethic, **reliability, and attendance** * Professional attitude with a **calm and customer\-focused approach** Apply via Indeed or contact us directly at: 0872183327 \- Adrian unitedtyreswalkinstown@gmail.com Job Types: Full\-time, Permanent Pay: €45,000\.00\-€50,000\.00 per year Benefits: * Employee assistance program * Employee discount * On\-site parking * Sick pay Experience: * Motor Mechanic: 3 years (required) Language: * English (preferred) Licence/Certification: * B Driving Licence (required) Work authorisation: * Ireland (required) Work Location: On the road

title: Network Analyst
description: **Job Title: Network Analyst** **Job Description** We are seeking an Asset Inventory \& CMDB Analyst to support a global cybersecurity programme focused on network asset identification, data inventory, and CMDB accuracy. This role will work closely with cybersecurity, IT, and site stakeholders to transform large volumes of raw device and network data into a structured, validated asset inventory within ServiceNow CMDB. This position has emerged due to the rapid expansion of cybersecurity initiatives, including network segmentation and monitoring, which have created a growing demand for manual data review, validation, and stakeholder coordination across multiple sites. **Responsibilities** * Review and analyse large datasets of network and device information extracted from cybersecurity tooling such as Armis and Excel exports. * Identify and classify assets, including purpose, ownership, and site relevance. * Engage with site\-based IT and operational teams to validate asset data and resolve gaps. * Build and maintain structured asset inventories using Excel and related tools. * Upload validated asset records into ServiceNow CMDB in collaboration with platform teams. * Track progress across multiple sites and support delivery against agreed milestones. * Highlight data quality issues and identify opportunities to streamline or improve inventory processes. **Essential Skills** * Basic understanding of networking concepts such as IP addresses, MAC addresses, and device types/behaviour. * Strong Excel and data\-handling skills. * Comfortable working with large, unstructured datasets. * Excellent communication and stakeholder engagement skills. * Ability to explain technical or data\-related findings clearly and patiently. * Strong attention to detail and a methodical working approach. * Comfortable coordinating across multiple teams and locations. **Additional Skills \& Qualifications** * Experience with ServiceNow CMDB or similar asset management systems. * Familiarity with Armis or other asset discovery/monitoring tools. * Asset or inventory management Experience. * Process improvement or automation mindset. * Advanced Excel skills (Power Query, VBA). * Basic project coordination Experience. Location Dublin, Ireland Trading as TEKsystems. Allegis Group Limited. Level 1, The Chase, Carmanhall Road, Sandyford, Dublin 18, Ireland. No. 909257\. TEKsystems is a company within the Allegis Group network of companies (collectively referred to as "Allegis Group"). Aerotek, Aston Carter, EASi, Talentis Solutions, TEKsystems, Stamford Consultants and The Stamford Group are Allegis Group brands. If you apply, your personal data will be processed as described in the Allegis Group Online Privacy Notice available at https://www.allegisgroup.com/en\-gb/privacy\-notices. To access our Online Privacy Notice, which explains what information we may collect, use, share, and store about you, and describes your rights and choices about this, please go to https://www.allegisgroup.com/en\-gb/privacy\-notices. We are part of a global network of companies and as a result, the personal data you provide will be shared within Allegis Group and transferred and processed outside the UK, Switzerland and European Economic Area subject to the protections described in the Allegis Group Online Privacy Notice. We store personal data in the UK, EEA, Switzerland and the USA. If you would like to exercise your privacy rights, please visit the "Contacting Us" section of our Online Privacy Notice at https://www.allegisgroup.com/en\-gb/privacy\-notices for details on how to contact us. To protect your privacy and security, we may take steps to verify your identity, such as a password and user ID if there is an account associated with your request, or identifying information such as your address or date of birth, before proceeding with your request. If you are resident in the UK, EEA or Switzerland, we will process any access request you make in accordance with our commitments under the UK Data Protection Act, EU\-U.S. Privacy Shield or the Swiss\-U.S. Privacy Shield.

title: Network Service Engineer
description: **Your role** The Network Service engineer is responsible for monitoring and maintaining Digital Realty's Interconnectivity Network infrastructure to ensure minimum network downtime and customer service impacts. Troubleshoot all network incidents and any customer reported services issue within agreed upon SLA. If needed, the engineer is responsible for coordinating with internal and external providers to troubleshoot and fix any network/customer service issues. **What you’ll do** * Real\-Time Monitoring: Proactively tracking network health, traffic, and performance metrics to prevent failures. * Trouble Ticket Support: Create and manage trouble tickets with our equipment vendors and our fiber providers and support customer facing trouble tickets (Cloud Connect, Service Exchange, DRIX, IP Bandwidth, Metro Connect (Dark Fiber), Metro Connect (Ethernet), Metro Connect (Managed Wave), Ports, Links, Connections, Virtual Router). * Incident Management \& Response: Detecting, analyzing, and resolving network outages, outages, or performance degradation. * Maintenance \& Updates: Support the installation, configuring, and updating hardware, and firmware, including patch management. * Reporting: Providing detailed analysis regarding network performance, availability and recommending infrastructure design and routing improvements. * Incident Management, Incident Diagnosis and Incident Resolution within SLA's, OLA's and KPI's, ensuring that all incidents are investigated in a timely manner. * Provide regular updates to ticketing systems while progressing incidents through to resolution. * Liaising and escalating with external suppliers and other Digital Realty departments to ensure minimum disruption and downtime on the network. Staying up to date with new network technology supported by the NOC. * **What you’ll need** * A degree in telecommunications or equivalent, relevant industry experience. * Experience working with Juniper, Ciena, and Cisco network equipment are a plus. * Professional certifications such as CCNA, JNCIA, or Optical Associate/Foundation are a plus. * Proven experience working within a Network Management or Operations Center environment in the Managed Services (Data or Voice), Telecoms, ISP, or Cloud Service Provider sectors. * Experience working within a Data Center (co\-location) environment is advantageous. * Hands\-on experience with network monitoring tools and trouble\-ticketing systems. * Strong understanding of TCP/IP networking fundamentals. * Proficient in LAN and WAN troubleshooting, including physical layer cabling issues in co\-location environments. * Working knowledge of IP/MPLS networks (experience with Juniper MX Series is desirable). * Working experience of Optical Ethernet and Transport Network. (Ciena 6500 and Waveserver AI desirable) * Working experience with Optical Ethernet and Transport Networks (experience with Ciena 6500 and Waveserver AI is desirable). * Solid understanding of routing principles. * Experience working with VMware and Linux\-based systems. * Familiarity with Microsoft Azure ExpressRoute, AWS Direct Connect, Google Cloud Interconnect, or other CSP connectivity services is an advantage. * Scripting knowledge in Python or experience with Ansible automation tools is a plus. * Excellent written and verbal communication skills and strong attention to detail.

title: Onshore Renewable Civil Engineer
description: The Onshore Renewable Civil group is a key part of Generation Engineering Delivery (GED) within Engineering and Major Projects. We provide a full range civil engineering services from feasibility stage through construction and ultimately completion of renewable infrastructure projects for ESB. As a Senior Civil Engineer, you will provide technical engineering services in the delivery of project works packages and advising on appropriate engineering solutions for various aspects of projects. You will work across a diverse range of renewable projects, with a particular focus on delivering ESB’s utility scale solar farm programme. You will also support the mentoring and professional development of junior engineers, sharing good practice and lessons learned across the group. Depending on business needs, the role may involve a full\-time assignment to a project site or working as part of a dedicated project team. **Key Responsibilities** * Fully embrace the ESB safety culture across all activities by taking ownership and demonstrating visible commitment to safety. * Provision of strong civil engineering technical expertise and leadership for challenging technical projects * Managing engineering works package(s) relating to the delivery of civil engineering works, site investigations, underground cable infrastructure projects, etc. as required including scoping, procuring, technical assessment, contract award and construction and contract management. * Building and manage relationships with internal and external stakeholders and manage client relationships in partnership with team leaders and project managers ensuring delivery of the client service and engage junior staff in continuous service improvement. * Manage and co\-ordinate all relevant civil, structural and environmental engineering activities required to successfully deliver projects to approved scope, budget and programme. * Providing technical support to ESB in its preparation of various submissions in respect of consenting, auctions, licensing, leasing, and scoping of various engineering packages. * Assist with mentoring and professional development of junior staff within the group including sharing knowledge and lessons captured on projects. **Essential** **Experience and Qualifications** * Minimum 5 years' relevant experience * NFQ Level 8 Civil Engineering Degree or equivalent required * Advanced specialist expertise and technical skills in civil engineering * Strong commercial and project management skills * Proven success managing major civil engineering projects for internal and external stakeholders * Ability to meet deadlines and handle multiple tasks * Self\-motivated, able to manage and prioritise work independently * Full driver's license and access to a car **Desirable** * Experience in administering FIDIC Conditions of Contract * Chartered status in a recognised professional engineering institution **Location** This position will be based in Engineering and Major Projects Office based in The Sidings, Grand Canal Dock, Dublin or Eastgate, Little Island, Cork office but travel to project locations is a key requirement of the role. ESB is committed to smart working – combining digital tools with a culture of trust and empowerment to enable flexible ways of working. The successful candidate may work flexibly within their jurisdiction with attendance at the workplace for in\-person collaboration on at least 2 days per week. All smart working arrangements are subject to manager approval and ongoing review, based on the operational requirements of the role, the team, and the individual. **Reporting To** Senior Team Leader within Generation Engineering Delivery **Why Work with Us?** * Opportunity to lead Ireland’s transition to net zero carbon future * Career development through mentoring and training * Corporate Social Responsibility Opportunities * Sports and Social Clubs * Networking opportunities * Credit Union * Generous Pension * Access to staff well\-being programmes * Generous parental leave entitlements * Strong values\-based and inclusive culture * Strong commitment to diversity, equity and inclusion * Great team environment working to our Core Values: Courageous, Caring, Driven and Trusted **Salary** €59,000 \- €70,000 per annum. **Closing Date** 12th May 2026 **Notes:** Your application will be held in reserve for 6 months should you be suitable for the role. The successful candidate will be required to evidence their right to work in the \<\<\<\< Republic of Ireland / UK \>\>\>\> prior to commencement of employment.

title: Operations Associate (FTC) - Grafton Street
description: **WHY JOIN ALO?** Mindful movement. It’s at the core of why we do what we do at ALO—it’s our calling. Because mindful movement in the studio leads to better living. It changes who yogis are off the mat, making their lives and their communities better. title: Oracle Flexcube Business Analyst (Contractor – 6 Months)
description: MUFG Investor Services is a trusted partner to many of the world’s largest public and private funds, providing asset servicing and operational solutions built for alternatives. With over $1 trillion in client assets under administration, we offer fund administration, banking, payments, fund financing, foreign exchange overlay, corporate and regulatory services, custody, business consulting, and more. Operating from 17 locations worldwide, we help clients mitigate risk, enhance efficiency, and navigate the operational complexities of today’s investment management landscape. As a division of Mitsubishi UFJ Financial Group (MUFG), one of the world’s largest financial institutions with approximately $3 trillion in assets, we combine deep expertise with the strength and stability of a leading financial institution. To learn more, visit us at www.mufg\-investorservices.com. **Job Description** As an Oracle Flexcube Business Analyst, you will play a critical role in helping MUFG Investor Services get more value from its core banking platform. We run Oracle Flexcube Universal Banking as the foundation of our banking operations – covering payments, treasury, foreign exchange, money markets, nostro reconciliation, SWIFT connectivity and more. The platform has grown significantly in capability and there is a real opportunity to close the gap between what Flexcube can do and what we currently use. You will work across IT and the business to identify standard features we have not yet activated, assess bespoke components built in earlier phases to determine whether they can be replaced by native functionality, and drive resolution of open configuration and change requests. You will work closely with the Banking Family Lead in IT and collaborate across Application Management, Banking Operations, and Product Management. You will also be a key point of contact with Oracle Support, coordinating on open tickets, change requests, and configuration queries – bringing clarity and momentum to engagements that have historically required significant back\-and\-forth. This is a hands\-on, delivery\-focused role that combines platform knowledge with strong stakeholder management and the ability to produce clear, well\-evidenced analysis and documentation. **Key Responsibilities** **Platform Capability Assessment** * Conduct a structured review of licensed Flexcube modules to identify features and configurations available but not currently activated or used. * Benchmark current usage across payments, standing instructions, foreign exchange, money markets, nostro reconciliation, and reporting against the full standard product capability. * Produce a prioritised inventory of unutilised capabilities with assessed business value and implementation complexity. **Bespoke Component Rationalisation** * Review customisations and bespoke components built in prior versions to determine whether equivalent standard functionality now exists in the current release. * Prepare structured assessments for each bespoke item: functional equivalence, migration complexity, risk, and recommended action (retire, retain, or replace). * Work with the Banking Family Lead and Application Management to validate findings and agree a rationalisation roadmap. **Backlog Management and Resolution** * Triage, document, and drive resolution of open items in the Flexcube Jira backlog, spanning configuration issues, change requests, business queries, and scripting requirements. * Distinguish clearly between items requiring Oracle involvement, items resolvable through internal configuration, and items needing formal change requests. * Maintain backlog hygiene: correct categorisation, clear ownership, and defined next steps for all items. **Oracle Engagement and Coordination** * Act as the primary coordination point for Oracle Support on open tickets and change requests, working alongside the Banking Family Lead and Application Management. * Prepare clear, well\-evidenced submissions to reduce round\-trip delays and accelerate resolution. * Track Oracle commitments, patch schedules, and responses, escalating where timelines slip. **Business and IT Collaboration** * Work with Product Management to translate business requirements into Flexcube configuration or change specifications. * Engage Banking Operations, Application Management, and project teams to gather requirements, validate solutions, and support UAT. * Produce high\-quality documentation: business requirements, functional specifications, solution designs, and training notes as required. **Reporting and Governance** * Provide regular progress updates to the Banking Family Lead on backlog status, capability findings, and Oracle engagement. * Flag risks and issues promptly, particularly where unresolved items represent operational or compliance exposure. **Qualifications** **You Have:** **Flexcube \& Core Banking Knowledge** * 5–8\+ years of hands\-on experience working with Oracle Flexcube Universal Banking as a business analyst, functional consultant, or implementation specialist. * Deep understanding of core banking operations including payments (domestic and cross\-border), SWIFT messaging, foreign exchange, money markets, and account management. * Experience engaging with Oracle Financial Services on support tickets, change requests, and product configuration – ideally including post\-upgrade optimisation work. * Ability to assess bespoke platform customisations objectively and build evidence\-based cases for rationalisation or replacement. **Business Analysis** * Proven ability to analyse and document complex functional requirements and translate them into clear specifications suitable for configuration or development. * Experience managing and triaging product backlogs, working across IT and business stakeholders to prioritise effectively. * Strong written communication skills; able to produce high\-quality documentation independently without extensive supervision. * Excellent stakeholder management – comfortable bridging technical IT teams and non\-technical business users simultaneously. **Technical Skills** * SQL proficiency – able to write and interpret queries to interrogate Flexcube data, validate configurations, and support analysis work. * Familiarity with Flexcube integration patterns (web services, REST/SOAP APIs) and data flows between Flexcube and downstream systems. * Comfortable working with Jira or similar backlog management tools. **Additional Information** Take a look at our careers site and you’ll find everything you’d expect from a career with the fastest\-growing business at one of the world’s largest financial groups. Now take another look. Because it’s how we defy expectations that really defines us. You’ll feel that difference in all kinds of ways. Our vibrant CULTURE. Connected team. Love of innovation, laser client focus, and next\-level LEARNING \& DEVELOPMENT. Oh, and we really walk the talk when it comes to HYBRID WORKING. So, why settle for the ordinary? Apply now for a Brilliantly Different career. We thank all candidates for applying; however, only those proceeding to the interview stage will be contacted.

title: Product Data Analyst
description: **About Phorest** Would you like to create the future of a product used by thousands of businesses and millions of consumers? Phorest Salon Software powers over 11,000 hair and beauty salons in Ireland, UK, Germany, Australia, Finland, USA, Canada, UAE and Netherlands and the platform processes 7 million appointments a month for hair and beauty consumers. Phorest is the number 1 salon software brand for premium independent salons in those markets and our next goal is to become a platform upon which other companies can build applications for premium salons. Our system touches every point of the salon experience. We have the in\-salon software desktop application, Phorest Go (the native staff application), online bookings website for salons, and custom native apps per salon. In a typical month, Phorest processes 3 million appointments and we send over 3 million SMS and 4 million emails. We process 200k online bookings per month and over 150 custom built white label native apps. ****The Opportunity**** As a Product Data Analyst at Phorest, you’ll be embedded at the heart of our product development process, turning data into clear, actionable insights that shape how we build and improve our software. Working closely with Product Managers, Designers, and Engineers, you’ll help the team understand user behaviour, measure the impact of product changes, and identify opportunities to deliver more value to salons and their clients. You’ll own product analytics end\-to\-end — from defining metrics and building dashboards to conducting deep\-dive analyses and presenting findings to stakeholders. This is a role for someone who is genuinely curious about how products are used, comfortable working in a fast\-moving agile environment, and confident translating complex data into decisions. ****What You'll Do**** * Partner with Product Managers and teams to define success metrics, instrument features, and measure the impact of product changes and releases. * Build and maintain Looker dashboards and explores that surface product usage, engagement, retention, and funnel metrics to stakeholders across the business. * Conduct deep\-dive analyses on user behaviour, feature adoption, and churn signals to surface actionable insights that inform product roadmap decisions. * Define and maintain a product metrics framework, ensuring consistency in how KPIs are measured and reported across teams. * Support A/B testing and experimentation, helping teams design experiments, interpret results, and make statistically sound decisions. * Collaborate with Data Engineering to ensure the right events and data points are captured, pipelines are reliable, and the data model supports product analytics needs. * Proactively monitor product metrics, flagging anomalies, trends, and opportunities to the relevant teams. * Contribute to data literacy across product teams, coaching team members on self\-serve analytics, dashboard use, and metric interpretation. * Support ad hoc analytical requests from product and leadership, delivering timely and rigorous analysis. ****Who You Are**** * 3\+ years of experience in data analytics, ideally with some of that time in a product analytics context or working directly alongside a product team. * Strong proficiency in SQL, with the ability to write complex queries across large datasets. * Hands\-on experience with Looker (LookML, dashboards, explores) — a core requirement for this role. * Solid understanding of the product development lifecycle and how data fits into agile ways of working. * Strong data visualisation and communication skills — you can tell a clear story with data for both technical and non\-technical audiences. * Analytical mindset with strong attention to detail and a rigorous approach to data accuracy. * Self\-starter who can manage competing priorities and work independently in a fast\-moving environment. **The Following Are Preferred But Not Required** * Experience in the hair \& beauty industry or a vertical SaaS business serving SMEs. * Experience with Python or R for more advanced analysis. **Benefits** 🧘 Your wellbeing is important to us \- we provide private healthcare, 2 Wellness Days, an employee assistance program and a free online GP service. 💰 As part of our Financial Wellbeing, we provide competitive Compensation, an Employee Share Purchase Scheme, Pension, Life Assurance, and Income Protection. 🚵🏿 We help you travel by providing a bike to work scheme as well as tax saver transport tickets. 🦸‍♀️ We support the women who work in Phorest by offering 2 weeks leave for Fertility Treatment, Pregnancy Loss and Menopause. 🍼 We care for your family and provide Enhanced Maternity and Paternity Benefits. 🌳 We grow our own timber! We provide a great learning environment and extensive development opportunities. We run development programs and provide access to many online resources including LinkedIn learning. 🏠 Moving house? Phorest employees get 3 moving days. Want to learn more about Phorest? Check out nothingventured.rocks for our blog and Insights on building an evergreen company from the team here at Phorest. **Phorest is an equal opportunity employer.** For this position, flexi\-time and working from home is possible. We are also open to remote work. Get in touch to ask for more information or to chat about your future with Phorest! *Research shows that while men apply to jobs when they meet an average of 60% of the criteria, women and other marginalised folks tend to only apply when they check every box. So if you think you have what it takes, but don't necessarily meet every single point on the job description, please still get in touch. We'd love to have a chat and see if you could be a great fit.*

title: Purchasing Administrator
description: **Above Us** Home to the iconic Instantor® Brand, Sanbra Fyffe is a market leader in the manufacturing, wholesale and distribution of plumbing supplies to the builder’s merchants trade for 90 years. At Instantor, our values aren’t just words on a page \- they shape how we work, support each other, and deliver for our customers. We’re looking for someone who shares these values and wants to thrive in a collaborative, customer\-focused environment. **Our Values** Teamwork: All departments within the business act as one team to make Sanbra Fyffe the best it can be. Customer Focus: We commit to meet and exceed our customer’s expectations, by placing the customer at the centre of all our decisions. Quality \& Reliability: We focus on doing things the right way, the first time; delivering on quality, on time and on cost. Expertise: We develop and manufacture solutions to provide our customers with maximum value. **Role Purpose** Due to strong growth within the business, we are now expanding our Purchasing Team and looking to hire a Purchasing Administrator. This is an excellent opportunity for an ambitious individual to join our experienced and expanding Purchasing Department at the company’s HQ in Santry, Dublin. **Key Responsibilities** * Provide administrative support to the Purchasing Team * Maintain a clear and accurate internal overview of all incoming container deliveries * Ensure all shipping \& freight documentation is accurate, compliant and communicated to the Freight Forwarder and Goods Inwards team in a timely manner * Maintain day\-to\-day communication with Freight Forwarder, ensure that all the container paperwork is processed and all containers are released in time to avoid surcharges * Manage payment requests with Finance department to ensure timely container release * Ensure the internal product database is accurate and up to date with PO due dates, supplier product pricing and create shipping lists for all incoming containers * Work closely with Goods Inwards team, managing urgent demands, arranging local \& international collections \& deliveries ensuring timely delivery * Goods receipt processing and investigating supplier account queries * Report to wider Purchasing Team on overdue Purchase Order dates and update ETAs accordingly to uphold ERP integrity * Attend monthly stock meetings with management. * Work within an experienced and expanding Purchasing department **Key Requirements** * A third level qualification; preferably in Supply Chain, Purchasing or Business * Some level of experience in a busy purchasing department * Proficiency in Microsoft Office suite (Word, Excel, PowerPoint etc.) * Confident manner and excellent communications skills both verbal and written * Team player * Excellent organisational skills and has a keen eye for detail * A desire to learn about our products, systems and customers * Ability to use your own initiative and make informed decisions **Benefits Package** * Competitive Salary * Employer Pension Contribution from start date * Employee Assistance Program (for you, your partner/spouse, and dependents 16\+) * Staff Discounts * Employer funded education support. If you require any accommodations or adjustments at any stage, please let us know. *We do not require assistance from Recruitment Agencies at this time.* Job Type: Permanent Pay: €30,000\.00\-€35,000\.00 per year Benefits: * Bike to work scheme * Company pension * Employee assistance program * Employee discount * On\-site parking Location: * Santry, County Dublin (preferred) Work Location: In person

description: **About PSC Biotech** **Who are we?** PSC Biotech is a leading Biotech Consultancy firm founded in 1996, headquartered in Pomona, California, USA, with Global operations in Ireland, India, Singapore, Australia and the US, serving 350 clients in more than 23 countries worldwide. title: Quantity Surveyor - 2026 Work Placement
description: Procurement \& Cost Management Europe Region DUB0000SN **Shape a future with purpose at Arup in Dublin or Cork.** Arup’s purpose, shared values and collaborative approach has set us apart for over 75 years, guiding how we shape a better world. **The Opportunity** Arup Ireland are seeking talented students to join our Procurement \& Cost Management team. We look for students who are ready to jump into the professional world; diverse individuals with inquisitive minds and a desire to challenge. You will work alongside skilled professionals and contribute to the success of real projects. You will also enjoy learning sessions with technical experts within your team, working on local projects and all kinds of social events. A role in the Procurement and Cost management team will include liaising with other multi\-disciplinary contributors within Arup for the successful outcome of real\-world projects. You will be required to deliver projects to the required technical standards in a timely manner, reporting to the team leader or project manager as appropriate. **As a Quantity Surveyor work placement student at Arup, you will:** * Assist senior team members in delivering construction projects dealing with payment certificates and claims. * Assist senior team members in the preparation of bills of quantities, ensuring compliance with required method of measurement. * Be proactive in communicating and working with the direct team as well as Arup colleagues from various disciplines for the successful delivery of tasks. * Follow instructions and/or use own initiative to achieve designated tasks in a timely manner. * Understand the required outputs/deliverables of a project or assignment and work efficiently to deliver them. * Develop relationships with colleagues, always being professional, presentable and articulate. * Adopt a flexible approach to working, supporting multiple teams across the buildings and infrastructure sectors **Does this role inspire you?** We're looking for students who are currently working towards RICS accredited, surveying\-based degree. We also value students who are: * Proactive, motivated and enthusiastic. Able to apply their own initiative to undertake tasks independently. * A team player, who can adopt flexible working practices to support the needs of the business. * Articulate, with both excellent written and verbal communication skills. * Logical in their approach to tasks, with the ability to interpret, understand and analyse data. * Output focused, always working with the objective in mind. * Commercially astute with strong numerical skills, demonstrating an attention to detail. * Proficient with Microsoft Office products and ideally Cost X (Although not necessary). **Why Arup?** At Arup, we believe in investing in our people and supporting their growth from day one. As a trust\-owned company, we're driven by our purpose and values, creating a collaborative and rewarding environment for our employees. **Benefits that work for you:** * **Professional Growth \& Development**: Kickstart your career with our structured program, benefit from continuous learning opportunities, and receive guidance from experienced mentors. * **Wellbeing and Culture:** At Arup we are committed to fostering a healthy working culture by offering a range of health and wellbeing benefits, including health insurance, flexible working schemes, wellness programmes and an Employee Assistance Programme. * **Equality, Diversity and Inclusion:** We strive to embed inclusion in our way of working, our interactions with one another and the projects we deliver. We are committed to creating an inclusive working environment for talented people of any background, based on merit, fairness and respect. At Arup, you belong to an extraordinary collective where individuality is encouraged. Our strength comes from how we respect, share, and connect our diverse experiences, perspectives, and ideas. You'll have the opportunity to do socially useful work that has meaning – to Arup, to your career, and to the clients and communities we serve. We welcome applications from talented individuals of all backgrounds, regardless of age (within legal limits), gender identity or expression, disability, ethnicity, religion, sexual orientation, or any other protected characteristic. We are committed to making our recruitment process and workplaces accessible. If you need any assistance or adjustments, please visit our Candidate Support page for contact information and assistance. We're happy to help. **Please note this role is only suitable for those who are in full time education and are looking for a placement for the summer months or for those who are required to complete a placement as part of their course requirements.** **If you have recently graduated, please apply to one of our '2026 Graduate Programme' roles.** \#IREIntern \#LI\-Onsite **Do you want to join us in shaping a better world?**

title: Receptionist – Front of House - Dun Laoghaire
description: **Who We Are** Truly Dental is a growing network of modern, state\-of\-the\-art dental practices across Ireland. With a strong focus on patient care, innovation, and community, we create a welcoming environment where both patients and staff feel supported. Our Dunlaoghaire clinic is part of this vision, bringing high\-quality dental services to the local community. **Fast Facts** * 14\+ clinics operating across Ireland * Serving thousands of patients each year with comprehensive dental care * Strong emphasis on career progression for our staff, from entry\-level roles to management positions **Purpose of the Vacancy** We are seeking a **Front of House Receptionist** to join our Dunlaoghaire team. This role is essential to ensuring a smooth patient experience by welcoming visitors, managing appointments, and supporting the clinic's busy operations. **Key Responsibilities** * Greet and welcome patients in a professional and friendly manner * Manage multiline phone system and respond to inquiries * Schedule and confirm appointments efficiently * Prepare patient charts and update records in a timely manner * Handle billing statements and process payments * Support clinic staff with administrative needs * Maintain a welcoming and organized reception area **Requirements: Skills \& Competencies** **Education** * High school diploma or equivalent required * Administrative or medical administration training is a plus **Experience** * Previous front desk or receptionist experience (preferably in a medical or dental environment) * Experience in customerfacing roles with high service standards **Knowledge \& Skills** * Strong command of Microsoft Office (Word, Excel, Outlook) * Excellent written and verbal communication in English * Ability to multitask in a fastpaced environment * Confident, assertive, and able to handle challenging situations with patients **Core Competencies** * Multitasking and organizational skills * Strong interpersonal communication * Resilience and ability to stand firm with demanding clients * Teamoriented with a proactive attitude **What We Offer** * Competitive hourly rate * Training provided on all internal systems and processes * Clear career progression pathways (Front of House Supervisor, Assistant Practice Manager, Practice Manager) * Supportive, friendly work environment with opportunities to grow within Truly Dental **Key Details before Applying** * Full time role * Schedule includes weekends and evening shifts * Fluent, confident English communication is a must (native or nearnative level)

title: Regulatory Reporting Analyst
description: ***🚀 We’re on a mission to make money work for everyone.*** We’re waving goodbye to the complicated and confusing ways of traditional banking. After starting as a prepaid card, our product offering has grown a lot in the last 10 years in the UK. As well as personal and business bank accounts, we offer joint accounts, accounts for 16\-17 year olds, a free kids account and credit cards in the UK, with more exciting things to come beyond. Our UK customers can also save, invest and combine their pensions with us. With our hot coral cards and get\-paid\-early feature, combined with financial education on social media and our award winning customer service, we have a long history of creating magical moments for our customers! We’re not about selling products \- we want to solve problems and change lives through Monzo ❤️ 📍Dublin \| 💰€45,000 \- €55,000 \+ incentive awards tied to your performance➕ benefits \| Hear from the team ✨ **⭐Our Finance team** Our Finance team is growing and we’re looking for a curious, adaptive and data\-focused Regulatory Reporting Analyst with a strong attention to detail to join our regulatory reporting team at Monzo EU (Ireland). The team is responsible for understanding our prudential regulatory reporting requirements, working on projects to set up and submit these and collaborating with teams across the Group. **🔑You’ll play a key role by...** * Setting up and operating processes to produce timely, accurate and complete regulatory reporting and Pillar 3 disclosures for Monzo’s regulators (and the associated management information required for sign\-off) e.g. Central Bank of Ireland * Understanding, reviewing and documenting the applicable regulatory interpretations, such as the Capital Requirements Regulations (CRR) and Basel 3\.1 * Designing and implementing controls to ensure regulatory reporting is timely, accurate and complete and that regulatory compliance is maintained * Facilitating appropriate governance over regulatory reporting and ensuring compliance with the applicable control framework * Implementation and testing (UAT) of new releases of the Bank’s automated regulatory reporting tool * Partnering with teams internally to help them make informed business decisions and providing regulatory reporting input into new product launches * Ensuring any regulatory reporting you’re responsible for runs like clockwork, including leveraging data visualisation tools to build dashboards and facilitate useful insights * Designing brilliant ways to automate your work and leverage technology to ensure we scale with the business **🤩We’d love to hear from you if…** * You’re a qualified or part qualified accountant with experience working within financial services. * You have previous knowledge of regulatory reporting, such as capital reporting (COREP), liquidity reporting (e.g. LCR) or financial reporting (FINREP) * You’re excited by data and have a sharp eye for detail, with amazing Excel or GSheets skills * You have experience with some coding languages like SQL would be a bonus, although a willingness to learn is just fine * You have a proven record of implementing processes to deliver to non\-negotiable, regulatory deadlines * You have experience in the large\-scale implementation and testing (UAT) of automated reporting solutions * You want to automate or enhance recurring processes, particularly by leveraging technology, but know how to balance this with getting the actual reports out! * You have an open mind, enjoy transparency and think outside the box * What we’re doing at Monzo excites you! Not ticking every box? That’s totally okay! Studies show that women and people of colour might hesitate to apply unless they meet every single requirement. At Monzo, we’re dedicated to creating a diverse and welcoming team. If you’re passionate about this role and keen to learn and grow with us, we encourage you to apply— even if you don’t have everything thats listed just yet. Drop us your application, we’d love to hear from you! **🙌What’s In It For You** 💰€45,000 \- €55,000 ➕ incentive awards tied to your performance. ⏰We offer flexible working hours and trust you to work enough hours to do your job well, and at times that suit you and your team. 🏝 Annual Leave \- 34 days including public holidays (24 holiday days \+ 10 public holidays) 📚€1,200 learning budget each year to use on books, training courses and conferences. 🏥Private healthcare scheme 💰Pension scheme: the minimum contribution is 4% and Monzo matches any additional contributions that you make up to a maximum of 6% 💛Wellbeing benefits: financial education, women’s and men’s health support, mental health benefits, including coaching and counselling **🌈 The application journey has 4 key steps** * An introductory call with a Monzo Recruiter * An initial call with the hiring manager * A series of technical and behavioural interviews This process should take around 2\-3 weeks \- your schedule is really important to us, so we promise to be as flexible as possible! We have some guidelines on using Artificial Intelligence (AI) to ace an application and interview at Monzo. You can read them here. We’ll only close this role once we have enough applications for the next stage. Please submit your application as soon as possible to make sure you don’t miss out. If you're successful in applying for this role, we'll work with you to find a start date. In some cases, there might be a delay in when you can be released from your current role so that we can make sure things continue to run smoothly. We’ll be sure to communicate this with you and keep you updated. S1 **Equal opportunities for everyone** Diversity and inclusion are a priority for us and we’re making sure we have lots of support for all of our people to grow at Monzo. At Monzo, we’re embracing diversity by fostering an inclusive environment for all people to do the best work of their lives with us. This is integral to our mission of making money work for everyone. You can read more in our blog, 2024 Diversity and Inclusion Report and 2024 Gender Pay Gap Report. We’re an equal opportunity employer. All applicants will be considered for employment without attention to age, ethnicity, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, family or parental status, national origin, or veteran, neurodiversity or disability status. If you have a preferred name, please use it to apply. We don't need full or birth names at application stage 😊

title: Release Engineer
description: **About Air Apps** ================== At Air Apps, we believe in thinking bigger—and moving faster. We’re a family\-founded company on a mission to create the world’s first AI\-powered Personal \& Entrepreneurial Resource Planner (PRP), and we need your passion and ambition to help us change how people plan, work, and live. Born in Lisbon, Portugal in 2018—and now with offices in both Lisbon and San Francisco—we’ve remained self\-funded while reaching over 100 million downloads worldwide. Our long\-term focus drives us to challenge the status quo every day, pushing the boundaries of AI\-driven solutions that truly make a difference. Here, you’ll be a creative force, shaping products that empower people across the globe. Join us on this journey to redefine resource management—and change lives along the way. **The Role** ============ As a **Release Engineer** at Air Apps, you will be responsible for ensuring the **smooth, reliable, and efficient release process** of our applications. You will oversee **versioning, build management, and deployment workflows**, ensuring that new features and updates are delivered seamlessly to users. Your role will involve **collaborating with development, QA, DevOps, and product teams** to streamline and automate release cycles, reduce risks, and maintain high standards for software delivery. * This is a **fully onsite position**, based at our office in Lisbon, where you will collaborate closely with cross\-functional teams in person and contribute to a dynamic and fast\-paced environment. **We are open to support with relocation efforts.** **Responsibilities** ==================== * Manage and optimize **release pipelines** to ensure smooth deployment of software updates. * Define and maintain **versioning strategies**, ensuring consistency across multiple environments. * Coordinate with **engineering, QA, and DevOps teams** to ensure timely and stable releases. * Automate and improve **build, release, and deployment processes** for efficiency and reliability. * Monitor and troubleshoot **release\-related issues**, ensuring minimal downtime. * Maintain documentation for **release workflows, rollback plans, and deployment strategies**. * Ensure compliance with **security, performance, and quality standards** in all releases. * Work with CI/CD tools (e.g., **Jenkins, GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, CircleCI**) to manage automated releases. * Implement and maintain **feature flagging strategies** to enable controlled rollouts. * Analyze release performance and drive **continuous improvements in deployment processes**. **Requirements** ================ * Around **3\+ years** of experience in **release engineering, DevOps, or build/release management**. * Experience with **CI/CD tools** (Jenkins, GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, CircleCI, ArgoCD). * Strong understanding of **version control systems** (Git) and **branching strategies (GitFlow, trunk\-based development)**. * Experience with **containerization (Docker, Kubernetes)** and cloud environments (AWS, Azure, GCP). * Knowledge of **scripting languages** (Bash, Python, or PowerShell) for automation. * Familiarity with **build systems** for mobile and web applications (Xcode, Gradle, Webpack). * Strong troubleshooting skills and ability to quickly resolve **build or deployment issues**. * Understanding of **release risk assessment and rollback strategies**. * Experience working in an **agile development environment** with fast\-paced release cycles. * Excellent collaboration and communication skills to coordinate across teams. **What benefits are we offering?** ================================== * **Apple hardware** ecosystem for work. * **Annual Bonus** * **Top\-tier Health and Life Insurance** for peace of mind. * **Transportation Budget** to support your commute needs. * **Coverflex** benefits package for meal allowances, well\-being, and more. * **Childcare** support. * **Air Conference** \- an opportunity to meet the team, collaborate, and grow together. * **Pension Fund** to support your long\-term financial planning. * **Urban Sports Club** membership to keep you active. * **Meals 100% free** at the hub. **Diversity \& Inclusion** ========================== At Air Apps, we are committed to fostering a diverse, inclusive, and equitable workplace. We enthusiastically welcome applicants from all backgrounds, experiences, and perspectives. We celebrate diversity in all its forms and believe that varied voices and experiences make us stronger. **Application Disclaimer** ========================== At Air Apps, we value transparency and integrity in our hiring process. Applicants must submit their own work without any AI\-generated assistance. Any use of AI in application materials, assessments, or interviews will result in disqualification. Compensation Range: €54K \- €67K
