title: Technical Support Engineer
description: We are a global team of innovators and pioneers dedicated to shaping the future of observability. At New Relic, we build an intelligent platform that empowers companies to thrive in an AI\-first world by giving them unparalleled insight into their complex systems. As we continue to expand our global footprint, we're looking for passionate people to join our mission. If you're ready to help the world's best companies optimize their digital applications, we invite you to explore a career with us! **Your opportunity** As a New Relic Technical Support Engineer, you'll have the unique chance to dive deep into our infrastructure products, helping customers navigate through challenging technical issues. At New Relic, we value continuous learning and skill enhancement to ensure we provide top\-tier support in the ever\-evolving world of infrastructure technology. We prioritize training, technical knowledge, and customer empathy, your growth prospects will be limitless. You’ll be at the forefront of supporting our APM suite, with a deep focus on distributed tracing, code\-level profiling, and monitoring microservices within Kubernetes and cloud\-native environments. Are you ready to step into the world of Application Performance Engineering and make a difference? **What you'll do** * Collaborate across teams to assist in solving complex technical customer problems within our APM product suite. * Display strong analytical skills and technical troubleshooting abilities to address customer issues effectively. * Provide outstanding customer service and demonstrate genuine empathy towards customers facing technical difficulties. * Work closely with our engineering teams to resolve intricate APM\-related issues. * Support New Relic customers with installation, configuration, and data exploration requests related to infrastructure technologies. * Advocate for customer needs by documenting feedback on feature requests and reporting bugs to our Product Organization to enhance the infrastructure component of the New Relic platform. * Continuously advance your skills through training and exposure to additional infrastructure features and technologies. **This role requires** * Proven experience delivering end\-user software support through multiple channels like email, phone, and social media. * Passionate about customer satisfaction and capable of delighting customers even in challenging situations. * Experience troubleshooting and optimizing application performance (APM) in enterprise environments, with a focus on distributed tracing, latency analysis, and error profiling. * Solid understanding of Linux\-based environments, including services, permissions, and file manipulation. * Proficient in containerization and orchestration—specifically Docker and Kubernetes—with experience monitoring microservices and understanding sidecar patterns or DaemonSets * Familiarity with web technologies and cloud hosting services like AWS, Azure, and Google Cloud. * Ability to collaborate seamlessly across teams or disciplines to troubleshoot and solve problems. * Prior experience in a DevOps, Software Engineering, or Technical Support role. **Bonus points if you have** * Experience with additional IaaS platforms like Azure or Google Cloud Platform. * Certifications in Java, Python, K8S. * Familiarity with New Relic products. * Comfort in analyzing and interpreting system/application logs. Please note that visa sponsorship is not available for this position. Fostering a diverse, welcoming and inclusive environment is important to us. We work hard to make everyone feel comfortable bringing their best, most authentic selves to work every day. We celebrate our talented Relics’ different backgrounds and abilities, and recognize the different paths they took to reach us – including nontraditional ones. Their experiences and perspectives inspire us to make our products and company the best they can be. We’re looking for people who feel connected to our mission and values, not just candidates who check off all the boxes. If you require a reasonable accommodation to complete any part of the application or recruiting process, please reach out to resume@newrelic.com. We believe in empowering all Relics to achieve professional and business success through a flexible workforce model. This model allows us to work in a variety of workplaces that best support our success, including fully office\-based, fully remote, or hybrid. **Our hiring process** In compliance with applicable law, all persons hired will be required to verify identity and eligibility to work and to complete employment eligibility verification. Note: Our stewardship of the data of thousands of customers means that a criminal background check is required to join New Relic. We will consider qualified applicants with arrest and conviction records based on individual circumstances and in accordance with applicable law including, but not limited to, the San Francisco Fair Chance Ordinance. Headhunters and recruitment agencies may not submit resumes/CVs through this website or directly to managers. New Relic does not accept unsolicited headhunter and agency resumes, and will not pay fees to any third\-party agency or company that does not have a signed agreement with New Relic. New Relic develops and distributes encryption software and technology that complies with U.S. export controls and licensing requirements. Certain New Relic roles require candidates to pass an export compliance assessment as a condition of employment in any global location. If relevant, we will provide more information later in the application process. Candidates are evaluated based on qualifications, regardless of race, religion, ethnicity, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender expression or identity, age, disability, neurodiversity, veteran or marital status, political viewpoint, or other legally protected characteristics. Review our Applicant Privacy Notice at https://newrelic.com/termsandconditions/applicant\-privacy\-policy

title: Tester
description: **Role Overview** We are seeking an experienced Test Analyst to join a delivery team working on a customer\-facing portal and associated CRM backend systems. The successful candidate will work closely with a highly experienced Test Manager, contributing to high\-quality testing across web and CRM platforms within a collaborative and intuitive testing environment. This role suits someone who is comfortable operating independently, can quickly understand requirements, and enjoys working within a structured but supportive test function. **Main Responsibilities** * Design, execute, and document test cases, test scripts, and test scenarios * Perform functional, system, integration, and regression testing * Carry out testing across web\-based applications and CRM systems * Actively participate in defect identification, logging, tracking, and re\-testing * Work closely with developers, business analysts, and the Test Manager to ensure quality outcomes * Contribute to continuous improvement of testing processes and standards * Create and validate test data and data integrity between front end and back\-end systems * Participate in User Acceptance Testing support * Assist in improving QA processes and documentation * Development and refinement of regression test packs * Support testing activities within an Agile / DevOps\-style delivery environment (where applicable) **Key Requirements** * 3\+ years’ experience in a professional Test Analyst / Software Tester role * ISTQB Qualified * Strong experience in web application testing * Proven experience testing CRM platforms * Solid understanding of the software testing lifecycle (STLC) * Experience working from business and technical requirements * Strong attention to detail with excellent communication skills * Excellent organization and time management skills **Nice to Have** * Exposure to Azure DevOps environments and CI/CD pipelines * Experience testing customer or partner\-facing portals * Familiarity with Agile delivery frameworks * Experience testing MS applications and systems (e.g. Power Pages, Power Apps, Dynamics 365 etc.) * Automation testing skills and experience * Experience testing in highly regulated organizations or within QA certified organizations (e.g. ISO 2500\) * Experience of the SDL in a public sector organization

title: Trainee Accountant
description: Job Summary Zurich Life Ireland (ZLAP) is recruiting for a Trainee Accountant to join the financial operations team, part of the Zurich finance team. This role is a controlled function under the CBI Fitness and Probity Standard. Your Role As a Trainee accountant your main responsibilities will include, but not necessarily be limited to, the following: * Daily cash management process \- monitoring \& calculating funding requirements for assigned bank accounts. * Processing of EFT \& cheque payments with responsibility for sourcing the required bank approvers and recording the entries to the relevant cashbooks. * Processing of returned payments, stale cheques \& placing stops on cheque payments. * Allocation of daily EFT receipts to the policyholder administration system, reconciliation of the prior day’s updates \& preparing notifications for large values. * Processing of Direct Debit runs \& returns along with the daily reconciliation of same. * Raising weekly \& monthly cashbook journals to the SAP system. * Responsibility for resolving assigned bank reconciliation differences. * Investigate and reply to any queries assigned to you from the Finops queries mailbox. * Other ad\-hoc duties. Your Skills \& Experience * Excellent accuracy \& attention to detail. * Ability to work in a team environment \& contribute to the performance of the team * Good communicator with excellent interpersonal skills * Well organised \& capable of working to tight deadlines * Ability to think clearly and logically * Intermediate level of problem\-solving skills * Good working knowledge of Microsoft excel \& outlook * Willingness to learn and take ownership of tasks * Interested in completing an accountancy qualification Additional Information Primary work location is Blackrock, Co.Dublin. If you value an exciting and varied working environment and meet the above requirements, then our recruiter is looking forward to receiving your application by clicking on the button “Apply online”. **Who we are** Zurich is one Ireland's leading insurance companies providing a wide range of general insurance and life insurance products and services. The company employs over 1,000 people across its locations in Dublin and Wexford. Zurich is a strong brand, Zurich in Ireland is part of Zurich Insurance Group, a leading multi\-line insurer that serves its customers in global and local markets. Our 55,000 employees worldwide form the basis of our success, helping our customers in 210 countries and territories to understand and protect themselves from risk. In order to deliver our services, we offer a competitive compensation package and interesting opportunities for further training \& development. As a Zurich employee you benefit from a multitude of advantages as well as a strong culture, characterized by acceptance, diversity and team spirit. At Zurich we are an equal opportunity employer. We attract and retain the best qualified individuals available, without regard to race/ethnicity, religion, gender, sexual orientation, age or disability. This role may be available part\-time or full time. This is because we want the best people for our roles, and we recognise that sometimes those people aren’t available full\-time. Please talk to us at interview about the flexibility you may need.

title: Trainee Accountant
description: **💼 Trainee Accountant 📍 South County Dublin 🕘 Full\-time \| Permanent 💰 Competitive salary \+ benefits** Our client is seeking a Trainee Accountant to join its Financial Operations team. This is an excellent opportunity for someone early in their accounting career who is eager to gain hands\-on exposure across core financial processes while working towards a recognised accountancy qualification in a structured and supportive environment. **🔍 The Role** * Manage day\-to\-day cash processes, including monitoring and assisting with funding requirements across multiple bank accounts * Process EFT and cheque payments, ensuring correct approvals and accurate cashbook postings * Manage returned payments, cancelled cheques and payment amendments * Allocate daily EFT receipts and update internal finance systems accordingly * Process Direct Debit collections and returns, including daily reconciliations * Prepare regular cashbook journals for posting to general ledgers systems * Investigate and resolve discrepancies arising from bank reconciliations * Respond to finance\-related queries and support ad\-hoc tasks as required **👤 The Candidate** * Strong attention to detail with a high level of accuracy * Comfortable working in a team\-oriented finance environment * Clear and confident communicator * Well organised with the ability to manage deadlines effectively * Logical approach to problem solving * Good working knowledge of Microsoft Excel and Outlook * Eager to learn, take ownership, and develop professionally * Interested in pursuing or completing an accountancy qualification **📩 Interested?** If you're looking for a varied role within a supportive finance team and want to build a long\-term career in accounting, we'd love to hear from you. Apply online to be considered.

title: Trainee/Graduate BER Assessor
description: Leinster Property Consultants is a well established building surveying and energy consultancy practice, delivering professional services to local authorities, approved housing bodies and private sector clients across Ireland, working across surveying, energy consultancy and estate agency services. This is a hybrid role, involving a mix of site based inspections, office based time, and remote working. Job Types: Full\-time, Permanent Pay: €30,000\.00\-€32,000\.00 per year Benefits: * Additional leave * Company car * Company events * Company pension * Free or subsidised travel * On\-site parking * Work from home Work Location: Hybrid remote in Ardee, County Louth Application deadline: 07/05/2026 Reference ID: BER Assessor Expected start date: 11/05/2026

title: Trust & Safety Associate - Slovenian
description: 🌍 **We’re hiring in Dublin!** **Accenture UK \& Ireland** is looking for a **Platform Experience New Associate – Slovenian Speaker** to join our **Trust \& Safety team** . This is an **entry\-level opportunity** for someone passionate about keeping online communities safe while gaining hands\-on experience in content moderation and digital platform operations. As a Platform Experience Associate, you will review and analyze user\-generated content — including text, audio, and video, to ensure compliance with client guidelines. You’ll investigate, escalate, and resolve flagged content, helping to maintain a safe online environment. This is meaningful work that may involve exposure to sensitive content, so **resilience and stress\-management skills are essential** . 📍 **Location:** Dublin, Ireland 🕒 **Contract:** Full\-time, 40h/week, 12\-month contract (with potential extension) 📌 **What you’ll do:** * Review, classify, and remove content according to client guidelines * Stay up\-to\-date with policies and platform changes * Investigate and resolve reported issues, escalating when necessary * Participate in process improvements and training programs * Engage in conversations on socially sensitive topics to help keep communities safe 📌 **What we’re looking for:** * Native or fluent **Slovenian** and strong English communication skills * Strong attention to detail and critical thinking abilities * Ability to work objectively, without personal bias, in line with client policies * Flexibility to work **rotating shifts** in a 24/7 environment, including weekends and holidays * Previous content moderation, customer service, or business process outsourcing experience is a plus * Passion for the internet, social media, and digital culture Accenture is an equal opportunities employer and welcomes applications from all sections of society and does not discriminate on grounds of race, religion or belief, ethnic or national origin, disability, age citizenship, marital, domestic or civil partnership status, sexual orientation or gender identity. **If you are required to work a night shift as part of this role, you are obliged to complete a night worker health assessment. Please note that it is a requirement of your continued employment, that you are fit to work night shifts and your employment may be terminated, if you are unable to do so.** **About Accenture** *Accenture is a global professional services company with leading capabilities in digital, cloud and security. Combining unmatched experience and specialized skills across more than 40 industries, we offer Strategy and Consulting, Interactive, Technology and Operations services — all powered by the world’s largest network of Advanced Technology and Intelligent Operations centers. We embrace the power of change to create value and shared success for our clients, people, shareholders, partners and communities. Visit us at accenture.com.* **Disclaimer** *Content moderation is meaningful work that may be challenging at times. Active participation in Accenture’s well\-being support program, designed specifically for the Trust \& Safety community, provides valuable skills to promote individual and collective well\-being.* *Due to the nature of the role, the user may be frequently exposed to flashing lights or contrasting light and dark patterns.* **INCLUSION AND DIVERSITY** *We are a global collective of diverse talents. You can contact us at accessIErecruitment@accenture.com.* **As part of the interview process, you will be required to complete an English language test and a language test in the relevant language for this role. Together, we drive positive change and deliver value to our clients, partners, shareholders, communities, and each other.We believe that delivering value requires innovation, and innovation thrives in an inclusive and diverse environment. We’re proud to be consistently recognized as one of the World’s Best Workplaces™.Join Accenture to work at the heart of change.* *Visit us at www.accenture.com* **Equal Employment Opportunity Statement** *We believe that no one should be discriminated against because of their differences. title: UCD BEACON CLINICAL TUTOR (MEDICINE)
description: **Job Description** **Job Title: UCD BEACON CLINICAL TUTOR (MEDICINE)** **Department: UCD Beacon Hospital Academy \& UCD School of Medicine** **Reports to:** Dr Ruth Pilkington, Director of UCD Beacon Academy, Beacon Hospital **Date: July 2026 (Changeover)** **Available as Full\-time (Tutor \+ Clinical) or Part\-time (Tutor only)** **Overall Purpose of Job** Beacon Hospital has an academic partnership with University College Dublin and one of our principle objectives within this partnership is to support the clinical education programme in the UCD School of Medicine. The clinical tutor role plays an instrumental role in identifying key teaching moments for our undergraduate students who are attached to specialities as part of their experiential learning. All clinical tutors are part of a vibrant community of academic staff who pass on their clinical experience and expertise to the next generation of medical professionals. **Key Responsibilities and Deliverables: Clinical Tutor (Medicine)** 1\.1\. Coordinate and participate in the teaching and training of the students in Beacon Hospital. 1\.1\.1\. Provide tutorial support and facilitate shared learning experiences for students while on placements. 1\.1\.2\. Provide lectures as part of the formal clinical curriculum and assist with the preparation of clinical examinations. 1\.1\.3\. Provide a level of supervision of clinical activity appropriate to the competence and experience of the individual student. 1\.1\.4\. Utilise a range of appropriate methods of teaching and assessment to demonstrate clinical learning opportunities \- i.e. OSCE style tutorials, problem\-based learning and bed\-side tutorials. 1\.2\. Liaise with relevant UCD personnel to ensure that clinical placements satisfy academic requirements and ensure integrated delivery of all courses. 1\.3\. Coordinate and partake in the orientation and appraisal of medical students attending Beacon Hospital. 1\.4\. Partake in the competency assessment of interns using the national intern competency framework tools within 30 working days of the intern's commencement date. 1\.5\. Demonstrate leadership by assisting in the professional and personal development of the students and Interns throughout their clinical placement at the Beacon Hospital through promotion of self\-learning, formal lectures and tutorials. 1\.6\. Act as a resource for students and Interns seeking specialty information and guidance. 1\.7\. Ensure that the relevant information about progress and performance is made available to the Beacon Hospital and UCD student placement coordinator. 1\.8\. Act and behave professionally and perform one's duties in a manner that optimises patient care and protects and enhances the reputation of the UCD Beacon Hospital Academy. 1\.9\. Adhere to Hospital Policies and Procedures e.g. JCI standards, Code Blue, Infection Prevention and Control, Falls Reduction and data protection. 1\.10\. Contribute to quality improvement; risk management initiatives and policy development so that the overall clinical education is of the highest quality and reflective of best practice. 1\.11\. Build upon the library of tutorial content available in the hospital. The tutor is required to prepare tutorials that can be used in each of the module s \- i.e. Surgery A/B and Clin Res 1, and that can be reproduced with ease each year by clinical tutors at the Beacon Hospital. These may include high level overviews of certain topics that can be taught to students with supporting artefacts, media, and learning tools. 1\.12\. Engage actively in research and scholarly activity as appropriate to the school or hospital. 1\.13\. Champion the student and Interns' participation in clinical audits to ensure that UCD Beacon Hospital Academy programmes meet quality and patient safety standards and any other applicable performance measures. 1\.14\. Report to the appointed clinical lead consultant within the Beacon in all matters relating to medical students and Interns in the manner requested by said clinical lead. **Person Specification** **Qualifications** Current relevant Full Irish Medical Council Registration. MRCPI/Membership of relevant college is desirable **Experience** Minimum 3 years postgraduate experience in an acute hospital setting. Experience in clinical education of students and junior staff. Experience in the effective management of a poor performing staff member and or student. Attendance at clinical tutor trainee days is required (if not complete the successful candidate will be given the opportunity to attend.) **Job Specific Competencies and Knowledge** High\-level interpersonal and communication skills (both oral and written) facilitating effective interaction within the work areas, the university and other institutions is a mandatory requirement. Strong organisational skills, ability to prioritise work, to work to deadlines and to use own initiative is a mandatory requirement. Proven experience of working in a quality assurance/improvement environment is a mandatory requirement. Proven ability to work as part of a team is required. Ability to embrace change in a dynamic environment, willingness to adopt new procedures and undertake further appropriate training as required Experience in teaching and examining at third level is desirable. Experience in curriculum development and the development of clinical education programmes is desirable. Evidence of research and peer reviewed publications is desirable. Good knowledge of university rules, regulations and procedures, especially those pertaining to examinations, registration and student welfare is required. Experience in research development at third level is desirable. Experience of general and academic administration is desirable. Display awareness and appreciation of the service users and the ability to empathise with and treat others with dignity and respect. Demonstrate sufficient clinical knowledge and evidence\-based practice to carry the out duties and responsibilities of the role. Demonstrate an ability to apply knowledge to best practice standards. All posts in Beacon Hospital require a high level of flexibility to ensure the delivery of an effective and efficient service. Therefore, the post holder will be required to demonstrate flexibility as and when required by their manager or hospital management. **This job description is intended to be an outline of the areas of responsibility and deliverables at the time of its writing. As the Hospital and the post holder develop, this job description may be subject to review in light of the changing needs of the Hospital.**

title: VacancyTitle: Multi-Skilled Engineer
description: Salary**Competitive**Location**Windmill Lane** *This is a* *permanent**,* *full time* *vacancy* *that will close* *in a month* *at 23:59 BST**.***My job** **Multi‑Skilled Engineer** We’re looking for a **Multi‑Skilled Engineer** to provide high‑quality engineering support, focusing on equipment reliability, continuous improvement, and minimising downtime across site operations. This is a hands‑on role for someone who enjoys problem‑solving, working across departments, and keeping production running safely and efficiently. **What You’ll Do** * Carry out **planned preventative maintenance (PPMs)** and reactive repairs * Diagnose and repair electrical and mechanical faults quickly and safely * Support production teams to improve efficiency and reduce downtime * Accurately record work, downtime and root cause analysis within CMMS * Identify and implement engineering and process improvements * Maintain hygiene, health \& safety and housekeeping standards * Support site and group engineering projects * Act as a role model, supporting apprentices and developing best practice **What You’ll Bring** * City \& Guilds Level 3 (or equivalent) in Engineering * Strong **electrical and mechanical fault‑finding** skills * Knowledge of pneumatics, hydraulics and engineering systems * Experience in **FMCG or manufacturing** (desirable) * A proactive, ‘can‑do’ attitude with strong problem‑solving skills * Ability to work independently, safely and as part of a team **The company** Pilgrim’s Europe produces some of the best\-known and most iconic brands in the UK and Ireland, including Fridge Raiders, Rollover, Denny, Richmond, Oakhouse and Moy Park, alongside a diverse range of industry leading own\-label products in categories including fresh pork, lamb and chicken, working with all the major retailers and food service outlets.
